title.zh: 卷三十六 鄭范陳賈張列傳
title.en: Volume 36: Biographies of Zheng, Fan, Chen, Jia, Zhang
title.raw: 卷三十六 鄭范陳賈張列傳 Volume 36: Biographies of Zheng, Fan, Chen, Jia, Zhang

sentences.zh: 鄭興字少贛，河南開封人也。

translations.literal: Zheng Xing, courtesy name Shaogan, was a native of Kaifeng in Henan.
translations.idiomatic: Zheng Xing, style Shaogan, came from Kaifeng in Henan commandery.

sentences.zh: 少學公羊春秋。

translations.literal: In youth he studied the Gongyang Spring and Autumn.
translations.idiomatic: As a youth he trained in the Gongyang Annals.

sentences.zh: 晚善左氏傳，遂積精深思，通達其旨，同學者皆師之。

translations.literal: In later years he excelled in the Zuo commentary, thereupon accumulated refinement and thought deeply, penetrated its purport, and fellow students all took him as teacher.
translations.idiomatic: Later he mastered the Zuo tradition, pondered it until its meaning opened, and his classmates deferred to him as their teacher.

sentences.zh: [一]天鳳中，[二]將門人從劉歆講正大義，[三]歆美興才，使撰條例﹑章句﹑傳詁，及校三統歷。

translations.literal: [One] In the Tianfeng era, [two] he led his disciples to follow Liu Xin in expounding the correct great meanings, [three] Xin admired Xing's talent and had him compile regulations, chapter divisions, transmitted glosses, and collate the Triple Concordance calendar.
translations.idiomatic: Under Wang Mang's Tianfeng reign he brought his pupils to Liu Xin's seminars on the great canons; Liu Xin, impressed by his ability, set him to drafting rules, section glosses, and collating the Triple Concordance calendar.

translations.literal: Four.
translations.idiomatic: Fourth gloss reference in the commentary sequence.

sentences.zh: 注[一]東觀記曰：「興從博士金子嚴為左氏春秋。

translations.literal: Note [one] The Eastern View record says: "Xing studied the Zuo Spring and Autumn under Erudite Jin Ziyan."
translations.idiomatic: Commentary: The Dongguan ji records that he read the Zuo Annals under Erudite Jin Ziyan.

sentences.zh: 」 注[二]王莽年也。

translations.literal: " Note [two] A year-title of Wang Mang.
translations.idiomatic: Note: Tianfeng was a Wang Mang reign period.

sentences.zh: 注[三]左氏義也。

translations.literal: Note [three] The meaning of the Zuo commentary.
translations.idiomatic: Note: That is, the Zuo tradition's reading of the classics.

sentences.zh: 注[四]說文曰：「詁，訓古言也。

translations.literal: Note [four] The Shuowen says: "Gu means to gloss ancient speech."
translations.idiomatic: Commentary: The Shuowen defines gu as explaining archaic wording.

sentences.zh: 」音古度反。

translations.literal: " The reading is gu, departing tone.
translations.idiomatic: The character is read gu in the departing tone.

sentences.zh: 三統歷，劉歆撰，謂夏﹑殷﹑周歷也。

translations.literal: The Triple Concordance calendar was compiled by Liu Xin and refers to the calendars of Xia, Yin, and Zhou.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin's Triple Concordance calendar treats the Xia, Shang, and Zhou calendrical systems.

sentences.zh: 更始立，以司直李松行丞相事，先入長安，松以興為長史，令還奉迎遷都。

translations.literal: When Gengshi was established, rectifier Li Song acting as chancellor entered Chang'an first; Song appointed Xing chief clerk and ordered him back to welcome the move of the capital.
translations.idiomatic: When Liu Xuan took the throne, Li Song as acting chancellor reached Chang'an first and made Zheng Xing his chief clerk, sending him back to escort the court's relocation.

sentences.zh: 更始諸將皆山東人，鹹勸留洛陽。

translations.literal: Gengshi's generals were all men from east of the mountains and together urged remaining at Luoyang.
translations.idiomatic: The Gengshi generals, all from the eastern plain, pressed to stay in Luoyang.

sentences.zh: 興說更始曰：「陛下起自荊楚，權政未施，[一] 一朝建號，而山西雄桀爭誅王莽，開關郊迎者，何也？

translations.literal: Xing persuaded Gengshi, saying: "Your Majesty rose from Jing and Chu, and governing power has not yet been applied; [one] in one morning you established the title, yet the heroes west of the mountains vie to kill Wang Mang and open the passes to welcome you in the suburbs—why is that?
translations.idiomatic: Zheng Xing argued: "You rose from the Nanyang region—still called Jing-Chu—before your rule had taken shape, yet the western warlords are killing Wang Mang and throwing open the passes to greet you; why?

sentences.zh: [二]此天下同苦王氏虐政，而思高祖之舊德也。

translations.literal: [Two] It is because the realm alike suffered under the Wangs' cruel government and longs for the old virtue of the high ancestor.
translations.idiomatic: Because the empire had endured the Wangs' cruelty and yearned for Han Gaozu's virtue.

sentences.zh: 今久不撫之，臣恐百姓離心，盜賊復起矣。

translations.literal: If now for long you do not comfort them, your servant fears the hundred surnames will lose heart and bandits will rise again.
translations.idiomatic: Fail to reassure them and the people will drift; rebels will rise again.

sentences.zh: 春秋書『齊小白入齊』，不稱侯，未朝廟故也。

translations.literal: The Spring and Autumn records 'Qi Xiaobai entered Qi' without calling him marquis, because he had not yet sacrificed at the ancestral temple.
translations.idiomatic: The Annals record Duke Huan of Qi entering Qi without the title "marquis" because he had not yet worshipped at the state temple.

sentences.zh: [三]今議者欲先定赤眉而後入關，是不識其本而爭其末，恐國家之守轉在函谷，[四]雖臥洛陽，庸得安枕乎？

translations.literal: [Three] Now those who debate wish first to settle the Red Eyebrows and afterward enter the passes—this is not knowing the root while contending over the branch; I fear the state's defense will shift to Hangu, [four] and though you lie at Luoyang, how can you use a pillow in peace?"
translations.idiomatic: Those who say "defeat the Red Eyebrows first, then enter Guanzhong" mistake twigs for roots—I fear the frontier of defense slides east to Hangu; even asleep in Luoyang you would not rest easy."

sentences.zh: 」[五]更始曰：「朕西決矣。

translations.literal: "[Five] Gengshi said: "I have decided on the west."
translations.idiomatic: Gengshi replied: "My mind is set on the west."

sentences.zh: 」拜興為諫議大夫，使安集關西及朔方﹑涼﹑益三州，還拜涼州刺史。

translations.literal: " He appointed Xing remonstrance grandee, sent him to pacify Guanxi and the three provinces of Shuofang, Liang, and Yi, and on return appointed him governor of Liang.
translations.idiomatic: He named Zheng Xing remonstrance grandee to pacify the northwest and the three border commands, then on his return made him governor of Liang.

sentences.zh: 會天水有反者，攻殺郡守，興坐免。

translations.literal: When there were rebels in Tianshui who attacked and killed the grand administrator, Xing sat in guilt and was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: Rebels in Tianshui murdered the prefect; Zheng Xing lost his post by association.

sentences.zh: 注[一]更始起南陽，南陽屬荊州，故曰荊楚也。

translations.literal: Note [one] Gengshi rose at Nanyang; Nanyang belonged to Jing province, hence it is called Jing and Chu.
translations.idiomatic: Note: Gengshi began at Nanyang, part of Jingzhou, hence "Jing-Chu."

sentences.zh: 注[二]山西謂陝山已西也。

translations.literal: Note [two] "West of the mountains" means west of the Shan pass.
translations.idiomatic: Note: "Shanxi" here means west of the strategic Shan ridge.

sentences.zh: 注[三]小白，齊桓公也。

translations.literal: Note [three] Xiaobai is Duke Huan of Qi.
translations.idiomatic: Note: Xiaobai is Duke Huan of Qi.

sentences.zh: 春秋「齊小白入於齊」。

translations.literal: The Spring and Autumn has "Qi Xiaobai entered Qi."
translations.idiomatic: The Annals line reads: Qi Xiaobai entered Qi.

sentences.zh: 公羊傳曰：「曷為以國氏？

translations.literal: The Gongyang commentary asks: "Why is he called by the state surname?
translations.idiomatic: Gongyang asks why the text uses the state name:

sentences.zh: 當國也。

translations.literal: He was taking charge of the state.
translations.idiomatic: Because he was assuming rule of Qi.

sentences.zh: 其言入何？

translations.literal: What does the word 'enter' mean?
translations.idiomatic: Why does it say "enter"?

sentences.zh: 篡辭也。」

translations.literal: It is the wording of usurpation.'"
translations.idiomatic: Gongyang answers: diction of usurpation."

sentences.zh: 注[四]言若不早都關中，有人先入，則國家鎮守轉在函谷也。

translations.literal: Note [four] This says that if you do not soon make the capital in Guanzhong and someone enters first, then the state's garrison shifts to Hangu.
translations.idiomatic: Note: Delay Guanzhong and another power holds Hangu first.

sentences.zh: 注[五]庸，用也。

translations.literal: Note [five] Yong means 'therefore.'
translations.idiomatic: Note: Yong here means "how then" or "therefore."

sentences.zh: 時赤眉入關，東道不通，興乃西歸隗囂，*[囂]*虛心禮請，而興恥為之屈，稱疾不起。

translations.literal: At that time the Red Eyebrows entered the passes; the eastern road was not open; Xing therefore went west to return to Wei Ao; Ao with empty mind courteously invited him, but Xing was ashamed to bend to him and pleaded illness without rising.
translations.idiomatic: When the Red Eyebrows blocked the east, Zheng Xing fled west to Wei Ao, who courted him humbly; Zheng Xing refused to serve him and stayed "ill" in bed.

sentences.zh: 囂鄉己自飾，常以為西伯復作，[一]乃與諸將議自立為王。

translations.literal: Ao had long since adorned himself and constantly thought the western lord was rising again; [one] he therefore discussed with his generals establishing himself as king.
translations.idiomatic: Wei Ao fancied himself a new King Wen of the west and plotted kingship with his generals.

sentences.zh: 興聞而說囂曰：「春秋傳云：『口不道忠信之言為嚚，耳不聽五聲之和為聾。

translations.literal: When Xing heard this he persuaded Ao: "The Zuo commentary says: 'The mouth that does not speak words of loyalty and trustworthiness is insolence; the ear that does not listen to the harmony of the five tones is deaf.
translations.idiomatic: Zheng Xing quoted the Zuo: "A mouth that will not speak loyalty is knavery; ears deaf to the five tones are useless.

sentences.zh: 』[二]閒者諸將集會，無乃不道忠信之言；

translations.literal: '[Two] Lately when the generals assembled, was it perhaps that they did not speak words of loyalty and trustfulness;
translations.idiomatic: At your last council, did anyone speak straight loyalty?

sentences.zh: 大將軍之聽，無乃阿而不察乎？

translations.literal: and is the great general's hearing perhaps biased and undiscerning?
translations.idiomatic: Or does your lordship hear only flattery?

sentences.zh: 昔文王承積德之緒，加之以睿聖，三分天下，尚服事殷。

translations.literal: Formerly King Wen inherited a thread of accumulated virtue, added to it sagely intelligence, held two-thirds of the realm, and still served the Yin.
translations.idiomatic: King Wen inherited deep virtue and sagacity, held two-thirds of the world, and still served Shang.

sentences.zh: [三]及武王即位，八百諸侯不謀同會，皆曰『紂可伐矣』，武王以未知天命，還兵待時。

translations.literal: [Three] When King Wu took the throne, eight hundred lords met without prior plan and all said 'Zhou may be attacked,' yet King Wu, because he did not yet know heaven's mandate, withdrew the army and awaited the time.
translations.idiomatic: When King Wu reviewed the host at Mengjin, eight hundred lords cried to strike Yin, yet he turned the army back until heaven's sign was clear.

sentences.zh: [四]高祖征伐累年，猶以沛公行師。

translations.literal: [Four] The high ancestor campaigned for successive years and still marched as lord of Pei.
translations.idiomatic: Even Han Gaozu fought for years under the modest title of lord of Pei.

sentences.zh: 今令德雖明，世無宗周之祚，威略雖振，未有高祖之功，而欲舉未可之事，昭速禍患，無乃不可乎？

translations.literal: Now though your ordered virtue is bright, the age has not the fortune of a Zhou house at its zenith; though your awesomeness and strategy are stirring, you have not the high ancestor's achievement—yet you wish to raise what cannot yet be done and thereby hasten calamity into the open—can this be acceptable?
translations.idiomatic: Your virtue shines, but there is no Mandate like Zhou's; your arms stir, but you are no Gaozu—yet you would rush what heaven has not licensed and invite disaster—can that be wise?

sentences.zh: 惟將軍察之。

translations.literal: Only let the general examine this.
translations.idiomatic: I beg you to weigh this.

sentences.zh: 」囂竟不稱王。

translations.literal: " Ao in the end did not take the kingly title.
translations.idiomatic: Wei Ao dropped the kingship plan.

sentences.zh: 後遂廣置職位，以自尊高。

translations.literal: Later he broadly set up offices to exalt himself.
translations.idiomatic: Later he still inflated his bureaucracy to aggrandize himself.

sentences.zh: 興復止囂曰：「夫中郎將﹑太中大夫﹑使持節官皆王者之器，非人臣所當制也。

translations.literal: Xing again checked Ao: "The general of the household for the center, grand palace counsellor, and bearer of credentials are all regalia of kings; they are not what a subject may institute.
translations.idiomatic: Zheng Xing warned again: general of the center, grand palace counsellor, and credential-bearing envoy are royal insignia, not a minister's to invent.

sentences.zh: 孔子曰：『唯器與名，不可以假人。

translations.literal: Confucius said: 'Only ritual vessels and titles may not be lent to others.
translations.idiomatic: Confucius said vessels and rank-titles must not be loaned out.

sentences.zh: 』[五]不可以假人者，亦不可以假於人也。

translations.literal: '[Five] What may not be lent to others may also not be borrowed from others.
translations.idiomatic: What must not be lent must not be borrowed either.

sentences.zh: 無益於實，有損於名，非尊上之意也。

translations.literal: It adds nothing to substance and injures the name; it is not what exalts superiors intends.
translations.idiomatic: It gains nothing real but costs reputation—hardly how to honor a sovereign.

sentences.zh: 」囂病之而止。

translations.literal: " Ao found this difficult and desisted.
translations.idiomatic: Wei Ao took the rebuke and stopped.

translations.literal: Six.
translations.idiomatic: Sixth gloss reference in the commentary sequence.

sentences.zh: 注[一]西伯，文王也。

translations.literal: Note [one] The western lord is King Wen.
translations.idiomatic: Note: "Western lord" means King Wen.

sentences.zh: 作，起也。

translations.literal: Zuo means 'to rise.'
translations.idiomatic: Zuo here means "to arise."

sentences.zh: 注[二]左傳富辰諫周襄王之辭。

translations.literal: Note [two] Words of Fu Chen remonstrating King Xiang of Zhou in the Zuo commentary.
translations.idiomatic: Note: From Fu Chen's remonstrance in the Zuo.

sentences.zh: 注[三]論語孔子曰：「三分天下有其二，以服事殷。」

translations.literal: Note [three] Confucius in the Analects says: "With two-thirds of the realm he still served Yin.'"
translations.idiomatic: Note: Analects on King Wen serving Yin despite holding two-thirds of the realm.

sentences.zh: 注[四]史記曰，武王觀兵孟津，諸侯不期而至者八百人，皆曰：「紂可伐矣。

translations.literal: Note [four] The Records say: King Wu inspected the army at Mengjin; eight hundred lords came without prior appointment and all said: "Zhou may be attacked.
translations.idiomatic: Note: Shiji on the Mengjin muster.

sentences.zh: 」王曰：「汝未知天命。

translations.literal: " The king said: "You do not yet know heaven's mandate.
translations.idiomatic: The king said you did not yet read heaven's will.

sentences.zh: 」乃還師。

translations.literal: " Thereupon he returned the army.
translations.idiomatic: So he marched the host home.

sentences.zh: 後聞紂殺比干，囚箕子，乃告諸侯以伐之。

translations.literal: Later he heard that Zhou had killed Bigan and imprisoned Jizi, and thereupon announced to the lords to attack him.
translations.idiomatic: When he learned that Zhou had killed Bigan and caged Jizi, he called the lords to war.

sentences.zh: 故曰待時也。

translations.literal: Therefore it is said he awaited the time.
translations.idiomatic: Hence "waited for the moment."

sentences.zh: 注[五]左傳杜預注曰：「器，車服；

translations.literal: Note [five] Du Yu's Zuo commentary note says: "Vessels mean chariots and robes;
translations.idiomatic: Note: Du Yu glosses "vessels" as chariots and regalia;

sentences.zh: 名，爵號也。

translations.literal: titles mean noble ranks.'
translations.idiomatic: titles as noble ranks."

sentences.zh: 」 注[六]病猶難也。

translations.literal: " Note [six] Bing here means 'to find difficult.'
translations.idiomatic: Note: Bing here means "to shrink from" or "find awkward."

sentences.zh: 及囂遣子恂入侍，將行，興因恂求歸葬父母，囂不聽而徙興捨，益其秩禮。

translations.literal: When Ao sent his son Xun to attend at court as hostage and was about to depart, Xing through Xun begged to return and bury his parents; Ao would not listen but moved Xing's quarters and increased his rank and courtesy.
translations.idiomatic: As Wei Ao sent his son hostage east, Zheng Xing asked leave via the son to bury his parents; Wei Ao refused, moved him to finer quarters, and raised his stipend instead.

sentences.zh: 興入見囂曰：「前遭赤眉之亂，以將軍僚舊，故敢歸身明德。

translations.literal: Xing entered to see Ao and said: "Formerly during the Red Eyebrow turmoil, because the general was my old colleague from office I dared entrust myself to your enlightened virtue.
translations.idiomatic: Zheng Xing went in and said: "In the Red Eyebrow chaos I came to you as an old colleague from Liang service.

sentences.zh: [一]幸蒙覆載之恩，復得全其性命。

translations.literal: [One] I have fortunately received the favor that covers and carries me and again have preserved my life intact.
translations.idiomatic: I owe you shelter and my life.

sentences.zh: 興聞事親之道，生事之以禮，死葬之以禮，祭之以禮，奉以周旋，弗敢失墜。

translations.literal: Xing has heard that the way of serving parents is to serve the living according to ritual, bury the dead according to ritual, and sacrifice according to ritual, upholding this in every turn and not daring to let it fall.
translations.idiomatic: The canon says: serve parents in life, death, and sacrifice by ritual—never slacken.

sentences.zh: [二]今為父母未葬，請乞骸骨，若以增秩徙捨，中更停留，是以親為餌，[三]無禮甚矣。

translations.literal: [Two] Now my parents are not yet buried; I beg to return my bones for burial; if by increasing rank and moving quarters you detain me midway, this is to take my parents as bait—[three] nothing could be more contrary to ritual.
translations.idiomatic: My parents lie unburied; if you keep me with promotions and a new house, you use my dead kin as bait—utterly against propriety.

sentences.zh: 將軍焉用之！

translations.literal: Of what use is the general!
translations.idiomatic: What use am I to you then?

sentences.zh: 」囂曰：「囂將不足留故邪？

translations.literal: " Ao said: "Is it that Ao is not worth retaining you?
translations.idiomatic: Wei Ao asked: "Am I not worth your staying?

sentences.zh: 」興曰：「將軍據七郡之地，[四]擁羌胡之觿，以戴本朝，德莫厚焉，威莫重焉。

translations.literal: " Xing said: "The general holds the territory of seven commanderies, [four] embraces the hosts of Qiang and Hu, and in supporting this court—no kindness is thicker, no awesomeness weightier.
translations.idiomatic: Zheng Xing answered: "You hold seven commanderies, command Qiang and Hu, and still honor Han—none is more loyal or more formidable.

sentences.zh: 居則為專命之使，入必為鼎足之臣。

translations.literal: At rest you are an envoy with sole commission; if you enter you will be a tripod leg among the ministers.
translations.idiomatic: At home you are plenipotentiary; at court you would be a pillar minister.

sentences.zh: 興，從俗者也，不敢深居屏處，因將軍求進，不患不達，因將軍求入，何患不親，此興之計不逆將軍者也。

translations.literal: Xing is one who follows custom; I do not dare to dwell deep in seclusion; through the general I sought advancement and did not worry about not reaching eminence; through the general I sought entry—why worry about not being intimate? This is a plan that does not go against the general.
translations.idiomatic: I am a worldly man—through you I sought office and need not fear obscurity; through you I sought the center and need not fear distance. None of this opposes your interest.

sentences.zh: 興業為父母請，不可以已，願留妻子獨歸葬，將軍又何猜焉？

translations.literal: Xing's task is to beg for my parents and cannot stop; I wish to leave wife and children and return alone to bury them—why should the general suspect?
translations.idiomatic: My only unmet duty is my parents' graves; let my family stay while I go alone to bury them—why doubt me?

sentences.zh: 」囂曰：「幸甚。

translations.literal: " Ao said: "Excellent.
translations.idiomatic: Wei Ao said: "Very well.

sentences.zh: 」促為辨裝，遂令與妻子俱東。

translations.literal: " He hastened to prepare travel gear and thereupon ordered him to go east with wife and children.
translations.idiomatic: He had baggage made ready and sent Zheng Xing east with his whole family.

sentences.zh: 時建武六年也。

translations.literal: It was the sixth year of Jianwu.
translations.idiomatic: This was Jianwu 6.

sentences.zh: 注[一]興嘗為涼州刺史，囂為西州將軍，故曰「僚舊」也。

translations.literal: Note [one] Xing had once been governor of Liang; Ao was general of the western provinces, hence 'old colleague from office.'
translations.idiomatic: Note: Zheng Xing had governed Liang while Wei Ao was "western provinces" general—hence "old colleague."

sentences.zh: 注[二]周旋猶遵奉也。

translations.literal: Note [two] Zhouxuan means to observe and uphold.
translations.idiomatic: Note: Zhouxuan means diligent observance.

sentences.zh: 左傳季文子曰「先大夫臧文仲教行父事君之禮，奉以周旋，弗敢失墜」也。

translations.literal: The Zuo commentary: Ji Wenzi said 'Former grandee Zang Wenzhong taught Xingfu the ritual of serving the ruler; I uphold it in every turn and do not dare to let it fall.'"
translations.idiomatic: Note: Zuo quotation on serving one's lord without slack.

sentences.zh: 注[三]猶釣餌也。

translations.literal: Note [three] The same as fishhook and bait.
translations.idiomatic: Note: "Bait" like on a hook.

sentences.zh: 注[四]七郡，天水、隴西、武威、張掖、酒泉、敦煌、金城也。

translations.literal: Note [four] The seven commanderies are Tianshui, Longxi, Wuwei, Zhangye, Jiuquan, Dunhuang, and Jincheng.
translations.idiomatic: Note: The seven commands from Tianshui through Jincheng.

sentences.zh: 侍御史杜林先與興同寓隴右，乃薦之曰：「竊見河南鄭興，執義堅固，敦悅詩書，[一]好古博物，見疑不惑，有公孫僑、觀射父之德，[二]宜侍帷幄，典職機密。

translations.literal: Censor-in-attendance Du Lin had formerly lodged with Xing west of the Long and therefore recommended him, saying: "Your servant has seen Henan Zheng Xing, who holds to righteousness firmly, is earnest in delighting in the Odes and Documents, [one] loves antiquity and broadly knows things, meets doubt without confusion, and has the virtue of Gongsun Qiao and Guan Shefu—he should attend within the curtain and manage secret business.
translations.idiomatic: Du Lin, who had shared quarters with him on the Long, recommended him: "Zheng Xing of Henan stands firm in duty, loves the classics, knows antiquity, resolves doubts, and has the stature of Zichan or Guan Shefu—fit for the privy council and secretarial work.

sentences.zh: 昔張仲在周，燕翼宣王，而詩人悅喜。

translations.literal: Formerly Zhang Zhong under the Zhou rejoiced and aided King Xuan, and the poet rejoiced.
translations.idiomatic: Under Zhou, Zhang Zhong aided King Xuan and the Odes praise him.

sentences.zh: [三]惟陛下留聽少察，以助萬分。

translations.literal: [Three] Only may Your Majesty pause to listen and examine slightly, to aid in the smallest degree.
translations.idiomatic: I beg you to heed him—it will help in ways you cannot measure.

sentences.zh: 」乃征為太中大夫。

translations.literal: " He was therefore summoned as grand palace counsellor.
translations.idiomatic: The court summoned him as grand palace counsellor.

sentences.zh: 注[一]左傳趙衰曰「臣亟聞卻縠之言矣，卻縠悅禮樂而敦詩書」也。

translations.literal: Note [one] In the Zuo commentary Zhao Cui said: 'Your servant has often heard Xi Hu's words; Xi Hu delights in ritual and music and is earnest in the Odes and Documents.'"
translations.idiomatic: Note: Zhao Cui's praise of Xi Hu from the Zuo.

sentences.zh: 注[二]左傳，子產辨黃熊，晉侯聞之，曰：「博物君子也。

translations.literal: Note [two] The Zuo commentary: Zichan explained the yellow bear; the marquis of Jin heard it and said: "A gentleman broad in things.
translations.idiomatic: Note: Zichan's yellow-bear gloss; the Jin ruler called him a learned gentleman.

sentences.zh: 」觀射父，楚大夫也，對楚昭王以重黎、羲和之事。

translations.literal: " Guan Shefu was a grandee of Chu who answered King Zhao of Chu on Chongli and Xihe.
translations.idiomatic: Guan Shefu was a Chu minister who briefed King Zhao on Chongli and Xihe.

sentences.zh: 見國語。

translations.literal: See the Discourses of the States.
translations.idiomatic: See Guoyu.

sentences.zh: 注[三]張仲，周宣王時賢臣也。

translations.literal: Note [three] Zhang Zhong was a worthy minister in the time of King Xuan of Zhou.
translations.idiomatic: Note: Zhang Zhong served King Xuan of Zhou.

sentences.zh: 燕，樂也。

translations.literal: Yan means 'joy.'
translations.idiomatic: Yan means "to delight in."

sentences.zh: 翼，敬也。

translations.literal: Yi means 'reverence.'
translations.idiomatic: Yi means "to aid reverently."

sentences.zh: 詩小雅曰：「侯誰在矣，張仲孝友。」

translations.literal: The Odes, Minor Ya, says: "Who is there?—Zhang Zhong, filial and friendly.'"
translations.idiomatic: Minor Odes: "Who is there? Zhang Zhong, filial and kind."

sentences.zh: 明年三月晦，日食。

translations.literal: On the last day of the third month the next year there was a solar eclipse.
translations.idiomatic: The next year, on the month's last day in the third month, the sun was eclipsed.

sentences.zh: 興因上疏曰：

translations.literal: Xing thereupon submitted a memorial saying:
translations.idiomatic: Zheng Xing then memorialized:

sentences.zh: 春秋以天反時為□，地反物為妖，人反德為亂，亂則妖□生。

translations.literal: The Spring and Autumn takes heaven's opposing the seasons as calamity, earth's opposing things as prodigy, and man's opposing virtue as disorder; when there is disorder then prodigies and calamities arise.
translations.idiomatic: The Annals treat heaven out of season as calamity, earth's anomalies as portents, and moral disorder as rebellion—rebellion breeds prodigies.

sentences.zh: [一]往年以來，□咎連見，意者執事頗有闕焉。

translations.literal: [One] From the previous year onward calamities and faults have appeared in succession; I suspect those in charge are somewhat lacking.
translations.idiomatic: Since last year omens have stacked up; I fear those who govern are at fault.

sentences.zh: 案春秋『昭公十七年夏六月甲戌朔，日有食之』。

translations.literal: Checking the Spring and Autumn: 'In the seventeenth year of Duke Zhao, summer, sixth month, jiaxu at the new moon, there was a solar eclipse.'
translations.idiomatic: The Annals record: Duke Zhao's seventeenth year, summer, sixth month, jiaxu new moon, eclipse of the sun.

sentences.zh: [二]傳曰：『日過分而未至，[三]三辰有□，[四]於是百官降物，[五]君不舉，[六]避移時，[七]樂奏鼓，[八]祝用幣，[九]史用辭。

translations.literal: [Two] The commentary says: 'When the sun has passed the equinox but not yet reached the solstice, [three] if the three luminaries show calamity, [four] then the hundred offices lower their standards, [five] the ruler does not hold full meals, [six] shifts the sitting for a time, [seven] music strikes the drum, [eight] invocators use silk, [nine] scribes use prayers.
translations.idiomatic: The gloss says: if the sun crosses mid-season without reaching solstice and the three lights show fault, officials lower banners, the ruler skips full meals, shifts audience hours, drums sound in the court, invocators offer silk, scribes read prayers.

sentences.zh: 』[一０]今孟夏，純干用事，陰氣未作，其□尤重。

translations.literal: '[Ten] Now in early summer pure yang is in charge and yin qi has not yet arisen; the calamity is especially heavy.
translations.idiomatic: Now it is early summer when yang rules alone; yin has not yet stirred—so the omen weighs heavily.

sentences.zh: 夫國無善政，則□見日月，變咎之來，不可不慎，其要在因人之心，擇人處位也。

translations.literal: When the state has no good government, then calamities appear in sun and moon; the coming of changing fault cannot but be treated cautiously; the essential point lies in following people's hearts and choosing men for their posts.
translations.idiomatic: Bad government shows in the lights of sky; such warnings demand care—above all, heed the people and place the right men in office.

sentences.zh: [一一]堯知鯀不可用而用之者，是屈己之明，因人之心也。

translations.literal: [Eleven] Yao knew Gun was unusable yet employed him—this was bending his own clear judgment to follow the hearts of men.
translations.idiomatic: Yao used Gun though he knew him unfit—bending his own judgment to heed the people's will.

sentences.zh: 齊桓反政而相管仲，晉文歸國而任卻縠者，是不私其私，擇人處位也。

translations.literal: Duke Huan of Qi returned power and made Guan Zhong minister; Duke Wen of Jin returned to the state and employed Xi Hu—this was not serving private interest but choosing the right men for office.
translations.idiomatic: Huan restored the government and raised Guan Zhong; Wen took the throne and picked Xi Hu—each put talent above private favor.

sentences.zh: [一二]今公卿大夫多舉漁陽太守郭伋可大司空者，而不以時定，道路流言，鹹曰「朝廷欲用功臣」，功臣用則人位謬矣。

translations.literal: [Twelve] Now the dukes and ministers mostly recommend Grand Administrator Guo Ji of Yuyang for grand minister of works yet do not settle the appointment in time; rumors on the roads all say 'the court means to reward military men'—once merit alone picks ministers, men and posts go awry.
translations.idiomatic: The court buzzes that Yuyang prefect Guo Ji may become grand minister of works; delay fuels gossip that only generals will be used—rewarding warriors with civil posts misplaces talent.

sentences.zh: 願陛下上師唐、虞，下覽齊、晉，以成屈己從觿之德，以濟腢臣讓善之功。

translations.literal: I hope Your Majesty above take as models Tang and Yu, below review Qi and Jin, to complete the virtue of humbling yourself to follow the multitude's mind, and to bring to fruition your ministers' work of yielding place to worthier men.
translations.idiomatic: Look up to Yao and Shun, look down at Qi and Jin: humble yourself to the people's judgment and let ministers step aside for better men.

sentences.zh: [一三] 注[一]左傳晉伯宗之辭。

translations.literal: Note one: words of Jin minister Bozong in the Zuo commentary.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary: this line echoes Bozong in the Zuo tradition.

sentences.zh: 天反時為□謂寒暑易節也。

translations.literal: Heaven opposing the seasons as calamity means cold and heat exchange their proper seasons.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven out of season means winter and summer trade places.

sentences.zh: 地反物為妖謂腢物失性也。

translations.literal: Earth opposing things as prodigy means creatures lose their proper nature.
translations.idiomatic: Earth's prodigy means things lose their inborn nature.

sentences.zh: 注[二]杜預注曰：「於周為六月，於夏為四月，純陽用事，陰氣未動而侵陽也。」

translations.literal: Note two: Du Yu's commentary says: 'In Zhou reckoning it is the sixth month, in Xia reckoning the fourth month; pure yang is in charge, yin qi has not yet stirred yet invades yang.'
translations.idiomatic: Du Yu notes the eclipse month: Zhou month six, Xia month four—yang rules but yin intrudes.

sentences.zh: 注[三]言過春分而未及夏至也。

translations.literal: Note three: this means having passed the spring equinox but not yet reached the summer solstice.
translations.idiomatic: Note: after spring equinox, before summer solstice.

sentences.zh: 注[四]三辰，日、月、星也。

translations.literal: Note four: the three luminaries are sun, moon, and stars.
translations.idiomatic: Note: the three lights are sun, moon, and stars.

sentences.zh: 注[五]降物，素服。

translations.literal: Note five: lowering standards means plain garments.
translations.idiomatic: Note: lowering banners means wearing undyed mourning dress.

sentences.zh: 注[六]不舉盛饌。

translations.literal: Note six: not holding full feasts.
translations.idiomatic: Note: the ruler skips lavish meals.

sentences.zh: 注[七]避正寢過日食時也。

translations.literal: Note seven: avoiding the main chamber until the eclipse hour has passed.
translations.idiomatic: Note: he vacates the main hall until the eclipse ends.

sentences.zh: 注[八]伐鼓。

translations.literal: Note eight: beating the drum.
translations.idiomatic: Note: the court drums during the rite.

sentences.zh: 注[九]用幣於社。

translations.literal: Note nine: presenting silk at the earth altar.
translations.idiomatic: Note: silk offerings at the soil altar.

sentences.zh: 注[一０]用辭以自責也。

translations.literal: Note ten: employing phrases of self-blame.
translations.idiomatic: Note: scribes read prayers of self-reproach.

sentences.zh: 此以上皆左傳載魯太史荅季平子之詞也。

translations.literal: All the foregoing is language from the Zuo commentary in which the grand scribe of Lu answers Ji Pingzi.
translations.idiomatic: The preceding glosses quote the Lu scribe's answer to Ji Pingzi in the Zuo.

sentences.zh: 注[一一]左傳晉士文伯曰「國無政，不用善，則自取□於日月之□，故政不可不慎也。

translations.literal: Note eleven: Shi Wenbo of Jin in the Zuo said: 'When the state has no good government and does not use the good, then of itself it draws calamity from the calamities of sun and moon—therefore government cannot be treated carelessly.
translations.idiomatic: Shi Wenbo warned Jin: bad rule draws celestial blame—government must be handled with care.

sentences.zh: 務三而已，一曰擇人，二曰因人，三曰從時」也。

translations.literal: Strive for three things only: first choose men, second follow the people's hearts, third follow the season.'"
translations.idiomatic: He named three tasks: pick the right men, heed the people, watch the seasons."

sentences.zh: 注[一三]濟，成也。

translations.literal: Note thirteen: ji means to complete.
translations.idiomatic: Note: ji here means to complete.

sentences.zh: 夫日月交會，數應在朔，而頃年日食，每多在晦。

translations.literal: When sun and moon meet, the reckoning ought to fall on the new moon, yet in recent years solar eclipses have mostly come on the month's last day.
translations.idiomatic: Conjunctions should fall on new moon, yet lately eclipses cluster on month-end.

sentences.zh: 先時而合，皆月行疾也。

translations.literal: They join ahead of time—all because the moon's motion is swift.
translations.idiomatic: They meet early because the moon runs fast.

sentences.zh: 日君象而月臣象，君亢急則臣下促迫，故行疾也。

translations.literal: The sun is the image of the ruler, the moon of ministers; when the ruler is proud and pressing, then inferiors are hurried and pressed, therefore their motion is swift.
translations.idiomatic: Sun stands for the sovereign, moon for his ministers; a hasty ruler drives subordinates faster than heaven's pace.

sentences.zh: 今年正月繁霜，自爾以來，率多寒日，[一]此亦急咎之罰。

translations.literal: This year in the first month there was heavy frost; since then most days have been cold—[one] this too is punishment for hasty fault.
translations.idiomatic: Heavy frost in the first month and a run of cold days are heaven's rebuke for rash rule.

sentences.zh: [二]天於賢聖之君，猶慈父之於孝子也，丁寧申戒，欲其反政，故□變仍見，此乃國之福也。

translations.literal: [Two] Heaven toward a sage and enlightened ruler is like a kindly father toward a filial son; it urges and warns repeatedly, wishing him to return to good government—therefore calamitous changes appear again and again; this is the state's good fortune.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven nags a wise ruler as a father corrects a dutiful son—omens repeat so he may mend policy; that is a blessing, not a curse.

sentences.zh: 今陛下高明而腢臣惶促，宜留思柔克之政，垂意洪範之法，[三]博采廣謀，納腢下之策。

translations.literal: Now Your Majesty is enlightened on high while your ministers are anxious below; it is fitting to ponder the policy of soft overcoming, turn your mind to the model of the Hong fan, broadly gather counsel, and adopt plans offered from below.
translations.idiomatic: You are clear-sighted while officials fret; dwell on the Hong fan's teaching of gentle mastery, cast a wide net for advice, and take counsel from the ranks.

sentences.zh: 注[一]正月，夏之四月。

translations.literal: Note one: the first month is the fourth month of the Xia calendar.
translations.idiomatic: Note: that "first month" is Xia fourth month.

sentences.zh: 注[二]書曰：「急恆寒若。」

translations.literal: Note two: the Documents say: 'Hasty constancy brings cold like.'"
translations.idiomatic: Note: the Documents tie harsh rule to unseasonable cold.

sentences.zh: 注[三]克，能也。

translations.literal: Note three: ke means able.
translations.idiomatic: Note: ke means capable.

sentences.zh: 柔克謂和柔而能立事也。

translations.literal: Soft overcoming means harmonious softness that can yet accomplish affairs.
translations.idiomatic: Soft mastery is supple government that still gets things done.

sentences.zh: 尚書洪範曰：「高明柔克。」

translations.literal: The Documents, Hong fan, says: 'Praising enlightenment, soft overcoming.'"
translations.idiomatic: The Hong fan praises a bright ruler who wins by softness.

sentences.zh: 書奏，多有所納。

translations.literal: When the memorial was submitted, much of it was adopted.
translations.idiomatic: The throne accepted most of his advice.

sentences.zh: 帝嘗問興郊祀事，曰：「吾欲以讖斷之，何如？

translations.literal: The emperor once asked Xing about suburban sacrifice and said: 'I wish to decide it by prognostications—how would that be?'
translations.idiomatic: The emperor asked about suburban rites: "Shall I settle them by weft-text prophecy?"

sentences.zh: 」興對曰：「臣不為讖。

translations.literal: Xing answered: 'Your servant does not deal in prognostications.'
translations.idiomatic: Zheng Xing replied: "I do not work with chenwei glosses."

sentences.zh: 」帝怒曰：「卿之不為讖，非之邪？

translations.literal: The emperor angrily said: 'That you do not deal in prognostications—is that to condemn them?'
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu flared: "Does refusing them mean you reject them?"

sentences.zh: 」興惶恐曰：「臣於書有所未學，而無所非也。

translations.literal: Xing in fear said: 'Your servant has not yet studied that material in the texts and does not condemn them either.'
translations.idiomatic: Zheng Xing said carefully: "I have not mastered those texts; I do not condemn them."

sentences.zh: 」帝意乃解。

translations.literal: The emperor's intent thereupon eased.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor's anger cooled.

sentences.zh: 興數言政事，依經守義，文章溫雅，然以不善讖故不能任。

translations.literal: Xing repeatedly spoke on government affairs, relying on the classics and holding to righteousness; his writing was mild and elegant, yet because he was not skilled at prognostications he could not be given heavy office.
translations.idiomatic: He often advised on policy from the classics, in polished prose, but never won high rank because he would not traffic in prophecy.

sentences.zh: 九年，使監征南、積弩營於津鄉，[一]會征南將軍岑彭為刺客所殺，興領其營，遂與大司馬吳漢俱擊公孫述。

translations.literal: In the ninth year he was ordered to supervise the expedition south and the accumulated crossbow camp at Jinxiang; [one] when expedition south general Chen Peng was killed by an assassin, Xing took command of his camp and thereupon with grand marshal Wu Han together attacked Gongsun Shu.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 9 he oversaw the southern expedition and crossbow encampment at Jinxiang; when Chen Peng fell to assassins, Zheng Xing took his troops and joined Wu Han against Gongsun Shu.

sentences.zh: 述死，詔興留屯成都。

translations.literal: When Shu died, an edict ordered Xing to remain garrisoned at Chengdu.
translations.idiomatic: After Shu's death he was ordered to hold Chengdu.

sentences.zh: 頃之，侍御史舉奏興奉使私買奴婢，坐左轉蓮勺令。

translations.literal: Before long the censor-in-attendance memorialized that on mission Xing had privately bought slave girls; he sat in guilt and was demoted to magistrate of Lianshao.
translations.idiomatic: Soon a censor charged him with buying concubines on public business; he was busted down to magistrate of Lianshao.

sentences.zh: [二]是時喪亂之餘，郡縣殘荒，興方欲築城郭，修禮教以化之，會以事免。

translations.literal: [Two] At that time in the aftermath of rebellion the commanderies and counties were ruined and desolate; Xing was about to build city walls and repair ritual instruction to transform the people, but was dismissed on account of affairs.
translations.idiomatic: The land was still shattered from war; he meant to wall the town and teach ritual, but another charge ended his tenure.

sentences.zh: 注[一]征南將軍岑彭、積弩將軍傅俊屯津鄉，以拒公孫述。

translations.literal: Note one: expedition south general Chen Peng and accumulated crossbow general Fu Jun were camped at Jinxiang to resist Gongsun Shu.
translations.idiomatic: Note: Chen Peng and Fu Jun held Jinxiang against Shu.

sentences.zh: 津鄉在今荊州也。

translations.literal: Jinxiang is in present-day Jingzhou.
translations.idiomatic: Jinxiang lay in what is now Jingzhou.

sentences.zh: 注[二]蓮勺，縣，屬左馮翊，故城在今同州下邽縣東北。

translations.literal: Note two: Lianshao, a county belonging to Zuo Fengyi; the old city is northeast of present Xiaji county in Tong prefecture.
translations.idiomatic: Note: Lianshao in Zuo Fengyi stood northeast of today's Xiaji in Tongzhou.

sentences.zh: 蓮音輦，勺音酌。

translations.literal: Lian is read nian, shao is read zhuo.
translations.idiomatic: Read lian as nian and shao as zhuo.

sentences.zh: 興好古學，尤明左氏、周官，長於歷數，自杜林、桓譚、□宏之屬，莫不斟酌焉。

translations.literal: Xing loved ancient learning, was especially clear on the Zuo commentary and Rites of Zhou, excelled at calendrical reckoning; from Du Lin, Huan Tan, Wei Hong, and their sort none failed to weigh his opinions.
translations.idiomatic: He loved antiquity, knew Zuo and Zhou li best, mastered astronomy; Du Lin, Huan Tan, and Wei Hong all deferred to his judgment.

sentences.zh: [一]世言左氏者多祖於興，而賈逵自傳其父業，故有鄭、賈之學。

translations.literal: [One] World talk says many who follow the Zuo tradition trace their lineage to Xing, yet Jia Kui transmitted his father's learning, hence there are Zheng and Jia schools.
translations.idiomatic: Later Zuo scholars often claim descent from Zheng Xing, while Jia Kui carried his father's line—hence the rival Zheng and Jia schools.

sentences.zh: 興去蓮勺，後遂不復仕，客授閿鄉，[二]三公連辟不肯應，卒於家。

translations.literal: Xing departed Lianshao and afterward did not again take office; he taught pupils as a guest in Wenxiang; [two] the three excellencies repeatedly summoned him and he would not respond; he died at home.
translations.idiomatic: He left Lianshao, never served again, taught in Wenxiang, refused every summons from the three dukes, and died at home.

sentences.zh: 子觿。

translations.literal: Son: Zhong.
translations.idiomatic: His son was Zheng Zhong.

sentences.zh: 注[一]斟酌謂取其意指也。

translations.literal: Note one: zhenzhuo means taking their essential meaning.
translations.idiomatic: Note: zhenzhuo means weighing the core sense.

sentences.zh: 注[二]閿音聞，古字也，建安中改作「聞」。

translations.literal: Note two: wen is read wen; it is an ancient character; in the Jian'an era it was changed to wen meaning 'hear.'
translations.idiomatic: Note: the place name used an old graph later written as "hear."

sentences.zh: 觿字仲師。

translations.literal: Zhong, courtesy name Zhongshi.
translations.idiomatic: Zheng Zhong, style Zhongshi.

sentences.zh: 年十二，從父受左氏春秋，精力於學，明三統歷，作春秋難記條例，兼通易、詩，知名於世。

translations.literal: At age twelve he received the Zuo Spring and Autumn from his father, concentrated his energy on study, understood the Triple Concordance calendar, composed regulations for a Spring and Autumn difficulty record, and was also versed in the Changes and Odes, becoming known in the world.
translations.idiomatic: At twelve he took Zuo from his father, mastered the Triple Concordance calendar, drafted rules for a Zuo critique, and knew the Changes and Odes—soon famous.

sentences.zh: 建武中，皇太子及山陽王荊，因虎賁中郎將梁松以縑帛聘請觿，欲為通義，引籍出入殿中。

translations.literal: In the Jianwu era the imperial heir and Prince Jing of Shanyang, through leader of the household gentlemen of the tiger guard Liang Song, with silks invited Zhong, wishing to compose comprehensive meaning, cite registers, and go in and out of the palace.
translations.idiomatic: The crown prince and Prince Jing tried to hire him through Liang Song of the tiger guard, offering silk to draft compendia and carry passes in the palace.

sentences.zh: 觿謂松曰：「太子儲君，無外交之義，漢有舊防，蕃王不宜私通賓客。

translations.literal: Zhong said to Song: 'The heir is the stored lord; there is no propriety of foreign diplomacy; Han has old prohibitions; a feudatory prince ought not privately to communicate with guests.'
translations.idiomatic: Zheng Zhong told Liang Song: "The heir apparent is the reserve sovereign; princes may not keep private clients; Han law forbids it."

sentences.zh: 」遂辭不受。

translations.literal: Thereupon he declined and did not accept.
translations.idiomatic: He declined.

sentences.zh: 松復風觿以「長者意，不可逆」。

translations.literal: Song again hinted to Zhong with 'an elder's intent may not be refused.'
translations.idiomatic: Liang Song pressed him with "elders must be obeyed."

sentences.zh: 觿曰：「犯禁觸罪，不如守正而死。

translations.literal: Zhong said: 'To violate prohibition and touch guilt is not as good as guarding rectitude and dying.'
translations.idiomatic: Zheng Zhong answered: "Better die upright than break the ban."

sentences.zh: 」太子及荊聞而奇之，亦不強也。

translations.literal: When the heir and Jing heard they thought him remarkable and did not force him.
translations.idiomatic: The prince and heir admired him and dropped the matter.

sentences.zh: 及梁氏事敗，[一]賓客多坐之，唯觿不染於辭。

translations.literal: When the Liang house affair failed, [one] many guests sat in guilt; only Zhong was not stained in accusation.
translations.idiomatic: When Liang Song fell, his clients were ruined; Zheng Zhong alone escaped implication.

sentences.zh: 注[一]梁松坐懸飛書誹謗下獄死，事見梁統傳也。

translations.literal: Note one: Liang Song sat guilty of posting flying letters slandering others, was imprisoned and died; the matter appears in the biography of Liang Tong.
translations.idiomatic: Note: Liang Song died in prison for forged libels; see Liang Tong's biography.

sentences.zh: 永平初，辟司空府，以明經給事中，再遷越騎司馬，[一]復留給事中。

translations.literal: At the beginning of Yongping he was summoned to the minister of works office, by clarity on the classics became gentleman consultant, was twice promoted to major of the swift cavalry, [one] again retained as gentleman consultant.
translations.idiomatic: Early in Yongping he entered the minister of works bureau, became a classics adviser, rose twice to swift-cavalry major, and stayed on as consultant.

sentences.zh: 是時北匈奴遣使求和親。

translations.literal: At that time the northern Xiongnu sent envoys seeking peace by marriage.
translations.idiomatic: The Northern Shanyu sought a heqin marriage.

sentences.zh: 八年，顯宗遣觿持節使匈奴。

translations.literal: In the eighth year Emperor Ming sent Zhong bearing credentials as envoy to the Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: In Yongping 8 Emperor Ming sent him north with the imperial staff.

sentences.zh: 觿至北庭，虜欲令拜，觿不為屈。

translations.literal: When Zhong reached the northern court, the barbarians wished to make him bow; Zhong would not bend.
translations.idiomatic: At the northern court they demanded a kowtow; he refused.

sentences.zh: 單于大怒，圍守閉之，不與水火，欲脅服觿。

translations.literal: The Shanyu was greatly enraged, surrounded and shut them in, gave neither water nor fire, wishing to coerce Zhong into submission.
translations.idiomatic: The Shanyu besieged the lodge, cut off fire and water, and tried to break him.

sentences.zh: 觿拔刀自誓，單于恐而止，乃更發使隨觿還京師。

translations.literal: Zhong drew his sword and swore an oath on himself; the Shanyu feared and desisted, then again sent an envoy who followed Zhong back to the capital.
translations.idiomatic: He drew his blade on his own throat; the Shanyu relented and sent him home with an escort.

sentences.zh: 朝議復欲遣使報之，觿上疏諫曰：「臣伏聞北單于所以要致漢使者，欲以離南單于之觿，堅三十六國之心也。

translations.literal: Court deliberation again wished to send an envoy to answer them; Zhong submitted a memorial remonstrating: 'Your servant has heard that the reason the northern Shanyu wants to bring Han envoys is to distance the southern Shanyu's allegiance and steel the hearts of the thirty-six states.'
translations.idiomatic: Court wanted to send another envoy; Zheng Zhong argued the Northern Shanyu only wanted to split the Southern Shanyu and harden the thirty-six western states.

sentences.zh: [二]又當揚漢和親，誇示鄰敵，令西城欲歸化者侷促狐疑，懷土之人絕望中國耳。

translations.literal: [Two] Moreover he will advertise Han's peace marriage and brag to neighboring foes, making those in the Western Regions who wish to submit hesitate and waver, and men who cherish their homeland despair of the Middle Kingdom.
translations.idiomatic: He would parade a Han marriage to neighbors, unsettle the Western Regions, and break the faith of men who lean toward China.

sentences.zh: 漢使既到，便偃蹇自信。

translations.literal: Once Han envoys arrive he will swagger and trust himself.
translations.idiomatic: Once Han envoys arrive he grows arrogant.

sentences.zh: [三]若復遣之，虜必自謂得謀，其腢臣駁議者不敢復言。

translations.literal: [Three] If we send them again, the barbarians will surely think they gained their scheme, and ministers who offered dissenting counsel will not dare speak again.
translations.idiomatic: Send again and the Xiongnu will think their plot worked; critics at court will fall silent.

sentences.zh: [四]如是，南庭動搖，烏桓有離心矣。

translations.literal: [Four] If so, the southern court will tremble and the Wuhuan will have hearts of departure.
translations.idiomatic: Then the Southern Shanyu wavers and the Wuhuan peel away.

sentences.zh: 南單于久居漢地，具知形埶，萬分離析，旋為邊害。

translations.literal: The southern Shanyu has long dwelt in Han territory and fully knows forms and power; should they split utterly apart, they will soon become a border harm.
translations.idiomatic: The Southern Shanyu knows Han's layout; if he breaks away he becomes a frontier scourge.

sentences.zh: 今幸有度遼之觿揚威北垂，雖勿報荅，不敢為患。

translations.literal: Now we are fortunate to have the army that crosses the Liao displaying might on the northern border; though we do not answer, they will not dare make trouble.'
translations.idiomatic: We already have the army beyond the Liao holding the north—even without a return embassy they will not dare move."

sentences.zh: 」[五]帝不從，復遣觿。

translations.literal: [Five] The emperor did not follow and again sent Zhong.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor overruled him and sent him again.

sentences.zh: 觿因上言：「臣前奉使不為匈奴拜，單于恚恨，故遣兵圍臣。

translations.literal: Zhong therefore presented a statement: 'When your servant previously went on mission he did not bow to the Xiongnu; the Shanyu harbored resentment and therefore sent troops to surround your servant.'
translations.idiomatic: Zheng Zhong wrote: "Last time I refused their bows; the Shanyu besieged me for it."

sentences.zh: 今復銜命，必見陵折。

translations.literal: Again bearing orders, I am sure to be insulted and crushed.
translations.idiomatic: A second mission means fresh humiliation.

sentences.zh: 臣誠不忍持大漢節對氈裘獨拜。

translations.literal: Your servant truly cannot bear to hold the great Han's staff alone and bow to felt robes.
translations.idiomatic: I cannot face the steppe alone with Han's staff and kowtow to fur caps.

sentences.zh: 如令匈奴遂能服臣，將有損大漢之強。

translations.literal: If it came to pass that the Xiongnu could make your minister submit, there would be damage to great Han's strength.
translations.idiomatic: If the Xiongnu forced a Han envoy to yield, the empire's prestige would suffer.

sentences.zh: 」帝不聽，觿不得已，既行，在路連上書固爭之。

translations.literal: The emperor did not listen; Zhong had no choice, and once departed on the road repeatedly submitted memorials firmly disputing it.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor insisted; en route Zheng Zhong fired off memorial after memorial protesting.

sentences.zh: 詔切責觿，追還系廷尉，會赦歸家。

translations.literal: An edict sharply rebuked Zhong, recalled him and bound him at the commandant of justice; he met an amnesty and returned home.
translations.idiomatic: An angry edict had him hauled back and jailed at the commandant of justice; an amnesty sent him home.

sentences.zh: 注[一]漢官儀曰「越騎司馬一人，秩千石」也。

translations.literal: Note one: Han guan yi says 'swift cavalry major, one man, rank one thousand shi.'
translations.idiomatic: Note: Han office tables list one swift-cavalry major at one thousand shi.

sentences.zh: 注[二]武帝開通西域，本三十六國。

translations.literal: Note two: Emperor Wu opened communication with the Western Regions, originally thirty-six states.
translations.idiomatic: Note: Wu opened the west to thirty-six states.

sentences.zh: 注[三]信音申。

translations.literal: Note three: xin is read shen.
translations.idiomatic: Note: read xin here as shen.

sentences.zh: 注[四]駁議謂勸單于歸漢。

translations.literal: Note four: boyi means ministers who urged the Shanyu to submit to Han.
translations.idiomatic: Note: boyi here means advisers who pushed the Shanyu toward Han.

sentences.zh: 注[五]明帝八年，初置度遼將軍，屯五原曼□。

translations.literal: Note five: in the eighth year of Ming they first established the general who crosses the Liao, stationed at Wuyuan Manyi.
translations.idiomatic: Note: the general who crosses the Liao was first posted at Wuyuan in Ming 8.

sentences.zh: 其後帝見匈奴來者，問觿與單于爭禮之狀，皆言匈奴中傳觿意氣壯勇，雖蘇武不過。

translations.literal: Later when the emperor saw Xiongnu who came he asked Zhong about contending etiquette with the Shanyu; all said that in the Xiongnu court Zhong's will and bearing were valiant, not even Su Wu was his better.
translations.idiomatic: Later, questioning Xiongnu visitors about the embassy, the court learned his defiance outshone even Su Wu's.

sentences.zh: 乃復召觿為軍司馬，使與虎賁中郎將馬廖擊車師。

translations.literal: He then again summoned Zhong as army marshal to join leader of the household gentlemen Ma Liao in attacking Cheshi.
translations.idiomatic: He recalled Zheng Zhong as army marshal to join Ma Liao against Cheshi.

sentences.zh: 至敦煌，拜為中郎將，使護西域。

translations.literal: Reaching Dunhuang, he was appointed general of the household for the center and sent to oversee the Western Regions.
translations.idiomatic: At Dunhuang he became general of the household for the center and protector of the Western Regions.

sentences.zh: 會匈奴脅車師，圍戊己校尉，觿發兵救之。

translations.literal: When the Xiongnu coerced Cheshi and besieged the colonels of the Wu and Ji garrisons, Zhong raised troops to rescue them.
translations.idiomatic: When the Xiongnu besieged the Wu-ji colonels at Cheshi, he marched to relieve them.

sentences.zh: 遷武威太守，謹修邊備，虜不敢犯。

translations.literal: Promoted grand administrator of Wuwei, carefully repaired border defenses, and the barbarians did not dare attack.
translations.idiomatic: As Wuwei prefect he tightened the frontier; the nomads did not strike.

sentences.zh: 遷左馮翊，政有名多。

translations.literal: Promoted Zuo Fengyi; his administration had a celebrated name on many counts.
translations.idiomatic: As Zuo Fengyi he governed so well that his name was praised everywhere.

sentences.zh: 建初六年，代鄧彪為大司農。

translations.literal: In the sixth year of Jianchu he replaced Deng Biao as grand minister of agriculture.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianchu 6 he succeeded Deng Biao as grand minister of agriculture.

sentences.zh: 是時肅宗議復鹽鐵官，觿諫以為不可。

translations.literal: At that time Emperor Zhang deliberated restoring the salt and iron offices; Zhong remonstrated that it was not acceptable.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Zhang debated reviving the salt and iron monopoly; Zheng Zhong opposed it.

sentences.zh: [一]詔數切責，至被奏劾，觿執之不移。

translations.literal: [One] Edicts repeatedly sharply rebuked him, until he was memorialized for impeachment; Zhong held firm and did not shift.
translations.idiomatic: Imperial edicts scolded him; he faced impeachment yet would not budge.

sentences.zh: 帝不從。

translations.literal: The emperor did not follow.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor still imposed the monopoly.

sentences.zh: 在位以清正稱。

translations.literal: In office he was praised for purity and integrity.
translations.idiomatic: His tenure was remembered for incorruptibility.

sentences.zh: 其後受詔作春秋刪十九篇。

translations.literal: Later he received an edict to compose the Spring and Autumn excisions in nineteen chapters.
translations.idiomatic: He was later ordered to write a nineteen-chapter abridgment of the Spring and Autumn.

sentences.zh: 八年，卒官。

translations.literal: In the eighth year he died in office.
translations.idiomatic: He died in office in the eighth year.

sentences.zh: 注[一]武帝時國用不足，乃賣鹽鐵，置官以主之。

translations.literal: Note one: in Emperor Wu's time state funds were insufficient; therefore salt and iron were sold and offices were set up to administer them.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary: Wu Di nationalized salt and iron when the treasury ran dry.

sentences.zh: 昭帝罷之，今議欲復之。

translations.literal: Emperor Zhao abolished them; now deliberation wishes to restore them.
translations.idiomatic: Zhaodi ended the monopoly; Zhang's court debated bringing it back.

sentences.zh: 子安世，亦傳家業，為長樂、未央廄令。

translations.literal: The son Anshi also transmitted the family enterprise and became stable commandant of Changle and Weiyang.
translations.idiomatic: His son Zheng Anshi inherited the learning and ran the imperial stables at Changle and Weiyang.

sentences.zh: [一]延光中，安帝廢太子為濟陰王，安世與太常桓焉、太僕來歷等共正議諫爭。

translations.literal: [One] In the Yan'guang era An Emperor deposed the heir apparent as prince of Jiyin; Anshi together with grand master of ceremonies Huan Yan, grand coachman Lai Li, and others jointly argued correct remonstrance.
translations.idiomatic: In Yan'guang, when Andi demoted the crown prince to prince of Jiyin, Zheng Anshi joined Huan Yan, Lai Li, and others in a principled protest.

sentences.zh: 及順帝立，安世已卒，追賜錢帛，除子亮為郎。

translations.literal: When Emperor Shun was enthroned, Anshi had already died; posthumously he was gifted cash and silk, and his son Liang was appointed gentleman.
translations.idiomatic: Shundi raised him posthumously with cash and silk and made his son Liang a court gentleman.

sentences.zh: 觿曾孫公業，自有傳。

translations.literal: Zhong's great-grandson Gongye has his own biography.
translations.idiomatic: Zheng Zhong's great-grandson Zheng Tai (style Gongye) has a separate chapter.

sentences.zh: 注[一]續漢志曰：「廄令一人，秩六百石。」

translations.literal: Note one: the continued Han treatise says: 'Stable commandant, one man, rank six hundred shi.'
translations.idiomatic: Note: the stable commandant ranked at six hundred shi.

sentences.zh: 范升字辯卿。

translations.literal: Fan Sheng, courtesy name Bianqing.
translations.idiomatic: Fan Sheng, style Bianqing.

sentences.zh: 代郡人也。

translations.literal: A man of Dai commandery.
translations.idiomatic: He came from Dai commandery.

sentences.zh: 少孤，依外家居。

translations.literal: In youth orphaned, he relied on his mother's family and dwelt with them.
translations.idiomatic: Orphaned young, he was raised by his mother's kin.

sentences.zh: 九歲通論語、孝經，及長，習梁丘易、老子，教授後生。

translations.literal: At nine he mastered the Analects and Classic of Filial Piety; when grown he studied the Liangqiu Changes and the Laozi and taught younger students.
translations.idiomatic: At nine he knew the Analects and Filial Piety; as an adult he read Liangqiu's Book of Changes and the Laozi and taught disciples.

sentences.zh: [一] 注[一]宣帝時梁丘賀之易也。

translations.literal: [One] Note one: the Changes of Liangqiu He in Emperor Xuan's time.
translations.idiomatic: Note: Liangqiu He's Changes school dates to Xuandi.

sentences.zh: 王莽大司空王邑辟升為議曹史。

translations.literal: Wang Mang's grand minister of works Wang Yi summoned Sheng as clerk of the deliberation bureau.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Yi, Mang's grand minister of works, made him a deliberation clerk.

sentences.zh: 時莽頻發兵役，徵賦繁興，升乃奏記邑曰：「升聞子以人不閒於其父母為孝，臣以下不非其君上為忠。

translations.literal: At that time Mang repeatedly launched military service and levies, and excises multiplied; Sheng thereupon submitted a memorandum to Yi saying: 'Sheng has heard that the son who lets no one come between him and his parents is filial, the minister who below does not blame his ruler and superior is loyal.
translations.idiomatic: As Mang piled on conscription and taxes, Fan Sheng wrote Wang Yi: "A filial son leaves no gap between himself and his parents; a loyal minister does not mutter blame against his ruler.

sentences.zh: [一]今觿人咸稱朝聖，皆曰公明。

translations.literal: [One] Now people everywhere call the court sagely and all say the duke is enlightened.
translations.idiomatic: Yet everyone calls the court sage and you, my lord, enlightened.

sentences.zh: 蓋明者無不見，聖者無不聞。

translations.literal: For the enlightened there is nothing unseen; for the sage there is nothing unheard.
translations.idiomatic: The enlightened see all; the sage hears all.

sentences.zh: 今天下之事，昭昭於日月，震震於雷霆，而朝雲不見，公雲不聞，則元元焉所呼天？

translations.literal: Now affairs of the realm are as clear as sun and moon, as startling as thunder and lightning, yet the court's clouds do not see and the duke's clouds do not hear—then where shall the common people cry to heaven?
translations.idiomatic: The empire's troubles shine like the sun—yet court and minister seem blind and deaf; where can the people turn?

sentences.zh: 公以為是而不言，則過小矣；

translations.literal: If the duke thinks it right yet does not speak, the fault is small;
translations.idiomatic: Stay silent though you know better, and the fault is small;

sentences.zh: 知而從令，則過大矣。

translations.literal: know yet follow orders, then the fault is great.
translations.idiomatic: know the harm yet obey, and the fault is great.

sentences.zh: 二者於公無可以免，宜乎天下歸怨於公矣。

translations.literal: Between these two the duke has no way to escape blame—it is fitting that the realm's resentment should return on the duke.
translations.idiomatic: You cannot escape blame for either; no wonder the world blames you.

sentences.zh: 朝以遠者不服為至念，升以近者不悅為重憂。

translations.literal: The court takes the non-submission of the distant as its chief care; Sheng takes the discontent of the near as his heavy worry.
translations.idiomatic: The court worries about distant rebels; I worry about the anger at your doorstep.

sentences.zh: 今動與時戾，事與道反，馳騖覆車之轍，探湯敗事之後，[二]後出益可怪，晚發愈可懼耳。

translations.literal: Now movement opposes the season and affairs run counter to the way; one gallops in the rut of the overturned cart and gropes boiling water after defeat—[two] the later one sets out, the stranger it is; the later one launches, the more fearful.
translations.idiomatic: Every policy runs against the times—you follow overturned tracks and scalded fingers; each late remedy looks odder and more frightening.

sentences.zh: 方春歲首，而動發遠役，藜藿不充，田荒不耕，谷價騰躍，斛至數千，吏人陷於湯火之中，非國家之人也。

translations.literal: It is the year's beginning in spring yet distant expeditions are stirred; coarse greens do not fill the belly, fields lie waste unplowed, grain prices leap, a hu reaches several thousand cash, clerks and people sink in boiling water and fire—they are not the state's true men.
translations.idiomatic: At New Year you launch distant campaigns while people starve, fields lie fallow, grain hits thousands per hu, and officials and folk burn together—those are not citizens of a well-ordered state.

sentences.zh: 如此，則胡、貊守關，青、徐之寇在於帷帳矣。

translations.literal: If it comes to this, then the Hu and Mo will hold the passes while the bandits of Qing and Xu sit within the curtain.
translations.idiomatic: If so, steppe peoples will hold the passes and the Qing-Xu rebels will sit inside your tent.

sentences.zh: [三] 升有一言，可以解天下倒縣，免元元之急，不可書傳，願蒙引見，極陳所懷。」

translations.literal: [Three] Sheng has one word that can release the realm from hanging upside down and relieve the common people's urgency; it cannot be written in a letter—I beg to receive a summons and set forth all that is in my heart.'"
translations.idiomatic: I have one remedy that could right the inverted world and ease the people—but it cannot go on paper; grant me an audience and I will speak plainly."

sentences.zh: 邑雖然其言，而竟不用。

translations.literal: Yi though he acknowledged his words in the end did not employ them.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Yi praised the letter yet ignored it.

sentences.zh: 升稱病乞身，邑不聽，令乘傳使上黨。

translations.literal: Sheng pleaded illness and begged to retire; Yi would not listen and ordered him to ride post to Shangdang.
translations.idiomatic: Fan Sheng begged sick leave; Wang Yi refused and sent him by post-horse to Shangdang.

sentences.zh: 升遂與漢兵會，因留不還。

translations.literal: Sheng thereupon joined Han troops and therefore stayed and did not return.
translations.idiomatic: He met Han forces and stayed with them.

sentences.zh: 注[一]論語孔子曰：「孝哉閔子騫，人不閒於其父母兄弟之言。

translations.literal: Note one: Confucius in the Analects said: 'How filial was Min Ziqian—others brought no separation between him and his parents and brothers.
translations.idiomatic: Note: Confucius on Min Ziqian's filial piety.

sentences.zh: 」閒，非也。

translations.literal: ' Jian means to blame.
translations.idiomatic: Jian means to find fault.

sentences.zh: 言子騫之孝，化其父母兄弟，言人無非之者。

translations.literal: It means Ziqian's filial piety transformed his parents and brothers so that people had nothing to blame in him.
translations.idiomatic: Min Ziqian's devotion left no opening for criticism.

sentences.zh: 忠臣事君，有過即諫。

translations.literal: The loyal minister serves the ruler: when there is fault he remonstrates.
translations.idiomatic: A loyal minister remonstrates when the ruler errs.

sentences.zh: 在下無有非君者，是忠臣也。

translations.literal: If below there is none who blames the ruler, that is a loyal minister.
translations.idiomatic: Loyalty means not privately blaming one's lord.

sentences.zh: 注[二]賈誼曰：「前車覆，後車誡。

translations.literal: Note two: Jia Yi said: 'The front cart overturns, the rear cart takes warning.'
translations.idiomatic: Note: Jia Yi's warning from overturned carts.

sentences.zh: 」論語曰：「見不善如探湯。」

translations.literal: The Analects says: 'Seeing evil is like touching boiling water.'"
translations.idiomatic: Note: the Analects likens evil to scalding water.

sentences.zh: 注[三]王莽時，青徐二部為寇，號「青徐賊」。

translations.literal: Note three: in Wang Mang's time the two departments of Qing and Xu became bandits, styled the Qing-Xu bandits.'
translations.idiomatic: Note: Mang's era saw the Qing-Xu insurgents.

sentences.zh: 建武二年，光武征詣懷宮，拜議郎，遷博士，上疏讓曰：「臣與博士梁恭、山陽太守呂羌俱修梁丘易。

translations.literal: In the second year of Jianwu Guangwu summoned him to the Huai palace, appointed him gentleman consultant, promoted him to erudite; he submitted a memorial declining: 'Your servant together with Erudite Liang Gong and Grand Administrator of Shanyang Lu Qiangang all cultivated the Liangqiu Changes.
translations.idiomatic: Jianwu 2: Guangwu summoned him to Huai, made him consultant then erudite; Fan Sheng demurred: "I studied Liangqiu with Erudite Liang Gong and prefect Lu Qiangang.

sentences.zh: 二臣年並耆艾，經學深明，而臣不以時退，與恭並立，深知羌學，又不能達，[一]籩負二老，無顏於世。

translations.literal: The two ministers are both aged in years, deeply clear in classical learning, yet your servant did not retire in due season and stands level with Gong; though I deeply know Qiangang's learning, I could not advance him—[one] I carry the two elders on my back and have no face in the world.
translations.idiomatic: Both men are old masters; I should have stepped aside for them yet hold rank beside Gong and failed to advance Qiangang—I shame two elders.

sentences.zh: 誦而不行，知而不言，不可開口以為人師，願推博士以避恭、羌。

translations.literal: To recite yet not practice, to know yet not speak—one may not open mouth and be others' teacher; I beg to yield the erudite post to yield place to Gong and Qiangang.'
translations.idiomatic: I cannot preach what I do not live; please give my chair to Gong and Qiangang."

sentences.zh: 」帝不許，然由是重之，數詔引見，每有大議，輒見訪問。

translations.literal: The emperor did not permit it, yet from this valued him, repeatedly issued edicts summoning audience, and whenever there was great deliberation always consulted him.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu refused but prized him thereafter, called him in often, and sought his counsel on great questions.

sentences.zh: 注[一]達，進也。

translations.literal: Note one: da means to advance.
translations.idiomatic: Note: da means to promote.

sentences.zh: 時尚書令韓歆上疏，欲為費氏易、左氏春秋立博士，[一]詔下其議。

translations.literal: At that time director of the secretariat Han Xin submitted a memorial wishing to establish erudites for the Fei lineage Changes and the Zuo Spring and Autumn; [one] an edict sent the matter down for discussion.
translations.idiomatic: Director Han Xin asked for chairs for Fei's Changes and the Zuo Annals; the emperor referred it to debate.

sentences.zh: 四年正月，朝公卿、大夫、博士，見於雲台。

translations.literal: In the fourth year, first month, the court convened dukes, ministers, grandees, and erudites at the Cloud Terrace.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 4, first month, he convened high ministers and scholars at the Cloud Terrace.

sentences.zh: 帝曰：「范博士可前平說。

translations.literal: The emperor said: 'Erudite Fan may come forward and state the case evenly.'
translations.idiomatic: The emperor said: "Erudite Fan, step forward and argue the case."

sentences.zh: 」升起對曰：「左氏不祖孔子，而出於丘明，師徒相傳，又無其人，且非先帝所存，無因得立。」

translations.literal: Sheng rose and answered: 'The Zuo does not trace its ancestry to Confucius but comes out of Qiu Ming; master and disciple transmitted it in succession, yet there is no such person, and moreover it was not preserved by former emperors—there is no basis for establishing it.'
translations.idiomatic: Fan Sheng replied: "Zuo does not stem from Confucius but from Zuo Qiu Ming, with no reliable line of teachers, and no earlier emperor endorsed it—so it should not get a chair."

sentences.zh: 遂與韓歆及太中大夫許淑等互相辯難，日中乃罷。

translations.literal: Thereupon with Han Xin and grand palace counsellor Xu Shu and others he mutually debated and disputed until midday before stopping.
translations.idiomatic: He wrangled with Han Xin, Xu Shu, and others until noon.

sentences.zh: 升退而奏曰：「臣聞主不稽古，無以承天；

translations.literal: Sheng withdrew and memorialized: 'Your servant has heard that if the ruler does not examine antiquity he has no means to receive heaven;
translations.idiomatic: He followed with a memorial: "Without reviewing antiquity a ruler cannot answer heaven;

sentences.zh: 臣不述舊，無以奉君。

translations.literal: if the minister does not transmit the old he has no means to serve the ruler.
translations.idiomatic: without handing down the old learning a minister cannot serve his lord.

sentences.zh: 陛下愍學微缺，勞心經蓺，情存博聞，故異端競進。

translations.literal: Your Majesty pities that learning is slightly deficient, labors the mind on the classics, and cherishes broad hearing—therefore divergent doctrines compete to advance.
translations.idiomatic: Your zeal for the classics invites rival schools to push forward.

sentences.zh: 近有司請置京氏易博士，腢下執事，莫能據正。

translations.literal: Lately the responsible officials requested establishing an erudite for the Jing lineage Changes, yet Your Majesty's agents could not uphold the correct position.
translations.idiomatic: When the court asked for a Jing Changes chair, no one held the orthodox line.

sentences.zh: 京氏既立，費氏怨望，左氏春秋復以比類，亦希置立。

translations.literal: Once the Jing lineage was established, the Fei lineage nursed resentment; the Zuo Spring and Autumn again by analogy also hoped to be established.
translations.idiomatic: Jing's chair angered Fei; Zuo then asked parity.

sentences.zh: 京、費已行，次復高氏，[二]春秋之家，又有騶、夾。

translations.literal: With Jing and Fei already in practice, next would come the Gao lineage; [two] among Spring and Autumn houses there are also Zou and Jia.
translations.idiomatic: After Jing and Fei would come Gao's Changes; the Annals also spawned Zou and Jia schools.

sentences.zh: [三]如令左氏、費氏得置博士，高氏、騶、夾，五經奇異，並復求立，各有所執，乖戾分爭，從之則失道，不從則失人，將恐陛下必有猒倦之聽。

translations.literal: [Three] If we let Zuo and Fei obtain erudites, Gao, Zou, and Jia—the strange divergences of the five classics—would all again seek establishment; each would hold its own grip, perverse and contending; to follow would lose the way, not to follow would lose men—I fear Your Majesty will surely have wearied listening.
translations.idiomatic: Grant Zuo and Fei chairs and five other odd schools will demand seats—each clinging to its sect. Grant them and the Way fragments; deny them and you lose scholars; you will weary of hearing pleas.

sentences.zh: 孔子曰：『博學約之，弗叛矣夫。

translations.literal: Confucius said: 'He who learns broadly and restrains himself with the rites will not depart from the way.'
translations.idiomatic: Confucius said: Learn widely, bind yourself with ritual, and you will not stray.

sentences.zh: 』[四]夫學而不約，必叛道也。

translations.literal: '[Four] Learning without restraint is sure to betray the way.
translations.idiomatic: Learning without restraint betrays the Way.

sentences.zh: 顏淵曰：『博我以文，約我以禮。

translations.literal: Yan Hui said: 'Broaden me with culture, restrain me with ritual.'
translations.idiomatic: Yan Hui asked to be widened by letters and tightened by rite.

sentences.zh: 』孔子可謂知教，顏淵可謂善學矣。

translations.literal: 'Confucius may be called to know teaching; Yan Hui may be called good at learning.
translations.idiomatic: Confucius knew how to teach; Yan Hui knew how to learn.

sentences.zh: 老子曰：『學道日損。

translations.literal: Laozi said: 'In learning the Way daily diminish.'
translations.idiomatic: Laozi said the Way is learned by daily stripping away.

sentences.zh: 』損猶約也。

translations.literal: ' Diminish means restrain.
translations.idiomatic: Diminish means distill.

sentences.zh: 又曰：『絕學無憂。

translations.literal: He also said: 'Cut off learning and there is no worry.'
translations.idiomatic: He also said: end petty learning and worry ceases.

sentences.zh: 』絕末學也。

translations.literal: ' Cut off means the trivial learning of later ages.
translations.idiomatic: Cut off means later pedantry.

sentences.zh: 今費、左二學，無有本師，而多反異，先帝前世，有疑於此，故京氏雖立，輒復見廢。

translations.literal: Now the Fei and Zuo two schools have no founding masters and mostly contradict and differ; former emperors in a prior age had doubts on this—therefore though the Jing lineage was established it was soon again abolished.
translations.idiomatic: Fei and Zuo lack founding sages and contradict each other; earlier emperors doubted them—Jing's chair was once set up then struck down.

sentences.zh: 疑道不可由，疑事不可行。

translations.literal: A doubtful way may not be followed; doubtful affairs may not be enacted.
translations.idiomatic: Do not walk a doubtful path or enact doubtful policy.

sentences.zh: 詩書之作，其來已久。

translations.literal: The making of the Odes and Documents is long since past.
translations.idiomatic: The Odes and Documents are ancient texts.

sentences.zh: 孔子尚周流遊觀，至於知命，自□反魯，乃正雅、頌。

translations.literal: Confucius still wandered and observed until he knew heaven's mandate, then from Wei returned to Lu and thereupon corrected the Ya and Song.
translations.idiomatic: Confucius wandered until he knew heaven's mandate, then returned from Wei to Lu and set the Ya and Song to rights.

sentences.zh: [五] 今陛下草創天下，紀綱未定，雖設學官，無有弟子，詩書不講，禮樂不修，奏立左、費，非政急務。

translations.literal: [Five] Now Your Majesty is founding the realm afresh; the great net is not yet fixed; though you have set up erudites there are no disciples, the Odes and Documents are not expounded, ritual and music are not cultivated—to memorialize establishing Zuo and Fei is no urgent task of government.
translations.idiomatic: You are still founding the dynasty—schools lack pupils, the Odes and Documents go untaught, ritual and music lie in ruins; Zuo and Fei are not the urgent business of rule.

sentences.zh: 孔子日：『攻乎異端，斯害也已。

translations.literal: Confucius said: 'Assault strange doctrines—therein lies harm.'
translations.idiomatic: Confucius warned against chasing heterodox arts.

sentences.zh: 』[六]傳曰：『聞疑傳疑，聞信傳信，而堯舜之道存。

translations.literal: '[Six] The commentary says: 'Hear doubt and transmit doubt, hear trust and transmit trust—and the way of Yao and Shun remains.'
translations.idiomatic: Tradition says: transmit only what is sure, mark what is doubtful—then Yao and Shun's path survives.

sentences.zh: 』[七]願陛下疑先帝之所疑，信先帝之所信，以示反本，明不專己。

translations.literal: '[Seven] I beg Your Majesty doubt what former emperors doubted, trust what former emperors trusted, to show return to the root and clarify that you do not trust yourself alone.
translations.idiomatic: Doubt what the Han founders doubted; trust what they trusted—show a return to roots, not private caprice.

sentences.zh: 天下之事所以異者，以不一本也。

translations.literal: The reason affairs of the realm differ is that they do not stem from one root.
translations.idiomatic: The realm splinters because learning has more than one trunk.

sentences.zh: 易曰：『天下之動，貞夫一也。

translations.literal: The Changes says: 'All movement under heaven is fixed by the one.'
translations.idiomatic: The Changes says all motion under heaven is anchored in the One.

sentences.zh: 』[八]又曰：『正其本，萬事理。

translations.literal: '[Eight] It also says: 'Set right the root and the myriad affairs are ordered.'
translations.idiomatic: Set the root straight and the myriad tasks align.

sentences.zh: 』[九]五經之本自孔子始，謹奏左氏之失凡十四事。

translations.literal: '[Nine] The root of the five classics begins with Confucius; respectfully I memorialize fourteen faults of the Zuo tradition.
translations.idiomatic: The five classics root in Confucius; I list fourteen Zuo errors.

sentences.zh: 」時難者以太史公多引左氏，升又上太史公違戾五經，謬孔子言，及左氏春秋不可錄三十一事。

translations.literal: " At that time objectors said the grand scribe often cited Zuo; Sheng further submitted that the grand scribe contradicted the five classics, falsely stated Confucius's words, and that the Zuo Spring and Autumn was unworthy to be recorded in thirty-one matters.
translations.idiomatic: Critics noted Sima Qian's Zuo citations; Fan Sheng added thirty-one counts where the Grand Historian clashes with the five classics and misquotes Confucius.

sentences.zh: 詔以下博士。

translations.literal: An edict sent it down to the erudites.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor sent the dossier to the erudites.

sentences.zh: 注[一]費直字長翁，善易，長於卦筮，見前書。

translations.literal: Note one: Fei Zhi, courtesy name Changweng, was skilled at the Changes and excelled at hexagram divination; see the former Han book.
translations.idiomatic: Note: Fei Zhi, expert in Changes divination, appears in Hanshu.

sentences.zh: 注[二]沛人高相善易，與費直同時，見前書。

translations.literal: Note two: Gao Xiang of Pei was skilled at the Changes, contemporary with Fei Zhi; see the former Han book.
translations.idiomatic: Note: Gao Xiang of Pei, Fei's contemporary, in Hanshu.

sentences.zh: 注[三]前書曰，騶氏無師，夾氏未有其書也。

translations.literal: Note three: the former Han book says the Zou lineage had no master, the Jia lineage had not yet its book.
translations.idiomatic: Note: Hanshu says Zou lacked a teacher and Jia lacked a text.

sentences.zh: 注[四]論語孔子之言。

translations.literal: Note four: words of Confucius in the Analects.
translations.idiomatic: Note: From the Analects.

sentences.zh: 弗叛言不違道也。

translations.literal: 'Not depart' means not to violate the way.
translations.idiomatic: Not departing means not leaving the Way.

sentences.zh: 注[五]孔子以魯哀公十一年自□還魯。

translations.literal: Note five: Confucius in the eleventh year of Duke Ai of Lu returned from Wei to Lu.
translations.idiomatic: Note: Confucius returned from Wei to Lu in Ai 11.

sentences.zh: 是時道衰樂廢，孔子來還，乃正之，故雅、頌各得其所。

translations.literal: At that time the way declined and music was abandoned; when Confucius came back he corrected them, therefore Ya and Song each obtained their proper place.
translations.idiomatic: The Way had decayed and music collapsed; his return set Ya and Song each in its place.

sentences.zh: 見史記。

translations.literal: See the Records of the Historian.
translations.idiomatic: See Shiji.

sentences.zh: 注[六]攻猶習也。

translations.literal: Note six: gong means to practice.
translations.idiomatic: Note: gong here means to drill in.

sentences.zh: 異端謂奇技也。

translations.literal: Strange doctrines mean odd skills.
translations.idiomatic: Heterodox doctrines mean occult tricks.

sentences.zh: 注[七]谷梁傳曰：「信以傳信，疑以傳疑。

translations.literal: Note seven: the Guliang commentary says: 'Trust is transmitted as trust, doubt as doubt.'
translations.idiomatic: Note: Guliang on transmitting certainty and doubt.

sentences.zh: 」公羊傳曰：「君子曷為春秋？

translations.literal: The Gongyang commentary says: 'Why did the gentleman make the Spring and Autumn?
translations.idiomatic: Gongyang asks why the gentleman composed the Annals:

sentences.zh: 樂堯舜之道也。」

translations.literal: He delighted in the way of Yao and Shun.'"
translations.idiomatic: to rejoice in the way of Yao and Shun."

sentences.zh: 注[八]易下系之文也。

translations.literal: Note eight: text from the lower wings of the Changes.
translations.idiomatic: Note: from the Changes' lower appendix.

sentences.zh: 注[九]今易無此文也。

translations.literal: Note nine: the present Changes has not this text.
translations.idiomatic: Note: that line is not in today's Changes recension.

sentences.zh: 後升為出妻所告，坐系，得出，還鄉里。
