translations.idiomatic: Kuaiji vied in virtue; thousands chanted the classics in the streets.

sentences.zh: 注[一]光祿勳之主事也，見漢官。

translations.literal: Note one: chief clerk to the minister of the imperial household; see Han offices.
translations.idiomatic: Note: Guangluxun chief clerk per Han gu.

sentences.zh: 初，霸以樊*

translations.literal: At first Ba, because Fan—
translations.idiomatic: Earlier Zhang Ba, because Fan Shu—

translations.literal: (shu)
translations.idiomatic: reading shu,

sentences.zh: **[鯈]*刪嚴氏春秋猶多繁辭，乃減定為二十萬言，更名張氏學。

translations.literal: **had trimmed Yan's Spring and Autumn which still had many verbose phrases, therefore reduced and fixed it to two hundred thousand graphs and renamed it the learning of the Zhang house.
translations.idiomatic: had condensed Yan Pengzu's Gongyang to two hundred thousand graphs and called it the Zhang school.

sentences.zh: 霸始到越，賊未解，郡界不寧，乃移書開購，明用信賞，賊遂束手歸附，不煩士卒之力。

translations.literal: When Ba first arrived in Yue, bandits were not yet pacified and the commandery borders were not quiet; he therefore sent letters opening rewards, clearly applying faithful bounties, and the bandits thereupon bound their hands and submitted without troubling the labor of troops.
translations.idiomatic: He pacified Yue with posted rewards; rebels surrendered without a fight.

sentences.zh: 童謠曰：「□我戟，捐我矛，盜賊盡，吏皆休。

translations.literal: A children's rhyme said: 'Stow my halberd, cast away my spear—the bandits are gone, the clerks may rest.'
translations.idiomatic: A rhyme ran: stow arms, rest clerks—the bandits were gone.

sentences.zh: 」視事三年，謂掾史曰：「太守起自孤生，致位郡守。

translations.literal: After three years in office he told his clerks: 'The grand administrator rose from solitary birth to reach commandery governor.
translations.idiomatic: After three years he told his staff: "I rose from nothing to this post.

sentences.zh: 蓋日中則移，月滿則虧。

translations.literal: The sun at midday then shifts; the moon at full then wanes.
translations.idiomatic: As the sun passes zenith it declines; the full moon wanes,

sentences.zh: [一]老氏有言：『知足不辱。

translations.literal: [one] Laozi has a saying: 'To know enough is not to be disgraced.'
translations.idiomatic: and Laozi said knowing when to stop avoids shame.

sentences.zh: 』」遂上病。

translations.literal: Thereupon he submitted illness.
translations.idiomatic: He then resigned on grounds of illness.

sentences.zh: 注[一]史記蔡澤之辭也。

translations.literal: Note one: these are words of Cai Ze in the Records.
translations.idiomatic: Note: Shiji, Cai Ze's image of rise and fall,

sentences.zh: 易豐卦曰「日中則昃，月盈則食」也。

translations.literal: The Changes, hexagram Feng, says 'When the sun is at midday it tilts; when the moon is full it is eaten.'"
translations.idiomatic: and the Feng hexagram on sun and moon.

sentences.zh: 後征，四遷為侍中。

translations.literal: Later he was summoned and after four promotions became attendant-in-chief.
translations.idiomatic: Recalled, he rose four steps to attendant-in-chief.

sentences.zh: 時皇后兄虎賁中郎將鄧騭，當朝貴盛，聞霸名行，欲與為交，霸逡巡不荅，觿人笑其不識時務。

translations.literal: At that time the elder brother of the empress, leader of the household gentlemen of the tiger guard Deng Zhi, was noble and powerful in court; hearing Ba's reputation and conduct he wished to befriend him, but Ba hesitated and did not answer—people of the time laughed that he did not know the times.
translations.idiomatic: Deng Zhi, the empress's brother, sought his friendship; Zhang Ba cold-shouldered him and contemporaries mocked him as obtuse.

sentences.zh: 後當為五更，會疾卒，年七十。

translations.literal: Later when he should have become one of the five elders, he met illness and died at age seventy.
translations.idiomatic: He was to be named a "five elder" but died ill at seventy.

sentences.zh: 遺□諸子曰：「昔延州使齊，子死嬴、博，因坎路側，遂以葬焉。

translations.literal: He left instructions to his sons: 'Formerly the envoy of Yan to Qi—when his son died at Ying and Bo, he buried him in a roadside pit there.
translations.idiomatic: His testament cited Yan's envoy burying a son by the road at Ying and Bo,

sentences.zh: [一]今蜀道阻遠，不宜歸塋，可止此葬，足藏發齒而已。

translations.literal: [one] now the Shu roads are blocked and distant; it is not fitting to return the tomb to the burial ground—stop here and bury; it is enough to cover hair and teeth.
translations.idiomatic: and said the road home to Shu was too long—bury him in Henan, bones only,

sentences.zh: 務遵速朽，副我本心。

translations.literal: Strive to follow quick rotting, matching my true mind.
translations.idiomatic: preferring a humble grave that rots fast,

sentences.zh: 人生一世，但當畏敬於人，若不善加己，直為受之。

translations.literal: In human life one generation, one should only fear and revere others; if ill is done to oneself, simply bear it.
translations.idiomatic: and urged sons to bear wrongs humbly rather than strike back.

sentences.zh: 」諸子承命，葬於河南梁縣，因遂家焉。

translations.literal: ' The sons obeyed the command, buried him in Henan in Liang county, and thereupon made their home.
translations.idiomatic: They buried him at Liang in Henan and settled there.

sentences.zh: 將作大匠翟酺等與諸儒門人追錄本行，謚曰憲文。

translations.literal: Superintendent of works Zhai Pu and others with the various Ru disciples recorded his original conduct and gave the posthumous title Xianwen.
translations.idiomatic: Zhai Pu and disciples gave him the posthumous title Xianwen.

sentences.zh: 中子楷。

translations.literal: The middle son was Kai.
translations.idiomatic: His second son was Zhang Kai.

sentences.zh: 注[一]嬴、博，二縣名，屬泰山郡。

translations.literal: Note one: Ying and Bo are two county names belonging to Taishan commandery.
translations.idiomatic: Note: Ying and Bo in Taishan,

sentences.zh: 禮記曰：「延陵季子適齊，其長子死於嬴、博之閒，因葬焉。」

translations.literal: The Record of Rites says: 'When Jizi of Yanling went to Qi, his eldest son died between Ying and Bo, and he buried him there.'"
translations.idiomatic: from the Liji story of Jizi of Yanling.

sentences.zh: 楷字公超，通嚴氏春秋、古文尚書，門徒常百人。

translations.literal: Kai, courtesy name Gongchao, mastered Yan's Spring and Autumn and the old-text Documents; disciples were constantly a hundred.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Kai, style Gongchao, knew Yan's Annals and old Documents with a hundred pupils.

sentences.zh: 賓客慕之，自父黨夙儒，偕造門焉。

translations.literal: Guests admired him; from his father's generation of old Ru down, they jointly came to his gate.
translations.idiomatic: Guests from his father's old circle flocked to him,

sentences.zh: 車馬填街，徒從無所止，黃門及貴戚之家，皆起捨巷次，以候過客往來之利。

translations.literal: Carriages and horses filled the streets; followers had nowhere to stop; families of the yellow gate and noble in-laws all built lodgings in the lanes to catch profit from passing visitors.
translations.idiomatic: filling the streets until eunuchs and in-laws rented lane booths for passing trade.

sentences.zh: 楷疾其如此，輒徙避之。

translations.literal: Kai hated this and repeatedly moved to avoid it.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Kai fled the circus by moving.

sentences.zh: 家貧無以為業，常乘驢車至縣賣藥，足給食者，輒還鄉里。

translations.literal: The family was poor and had no trade; he often rode a donkey cart to the county to sell medicine, and when he had enough to eat he returned to his village.
translations.idiomatic: Poor, he sold medicine from a donkey cart until fed, then went home.

sentences.zh: 司隸舉茂才，除長陵令，不至官。

translations.literal: The metropolitan commandant recommended him as flourishing talent; he was appointed magistrate of Changling but did not go to office.
translations.idiomatic: Recommended flourishing talent for Changling magistrate, he never took the post.

sentences.zh: 隱居弘農山中，學者隨之，所居成市，後華陰山南遂有公超市。

translations.literal: He lived in reclusion in the mountains of Hongnong; students followed him until where he dwelt became a market—later south of Huayin mountain there was a Gongchao market.
translations.idiomatic: He hid in Hongnong until followers formed a "Gongchao market" south of Huayin.

sentences.zh: 五府連辟，舉賢良方正，不就。

translations.literal: The five offices repeatedly summoned him and recommended him as worthy, upright, and possessed of the Way; he did not go.
translations.idiomatic: The five high bureaus summoned him as worthy and upright; he refused.

sentences.zh: [一] 注[一]五府，太傅、太尉、司徒、司空、大將軍也。

translations.literal: [One] Note one: the five offices are grand tutor, grand commandant, minister of education, minister of works, and general-in-chief.
translations.idiomatic: Note: the five top bureaus of state.

sentences.zh: 性好道術，能作五里霧。

translations.literal: His nature loved Daoist arts; he could make a fog for five li.
translations.idiomatic: He claimed to raise a five-li fog,

sentences.zh: 時關西人裴優亦能為三里霧，自以不如楷，從學之，楷避不肯見。

translations.literal: At that time Pei You of Guanxi could also make a fog for three li; he thought himself not equal to Kai and wished to study from him, but Kai avoided him and would not meet.
translations.idiomatic: while Pei You managed only three li and sought lessons; Zhang Kai refused to teach him.

sentences.zh: 桓帝即位，優遂行霧作賊，事覺被考，引楷言從學術，楷坐系廷尉詔獄，積二年，恆諷誦經籍，作尚書注。

translations.literal: When Emperor Huan took the throne, You then used fog to commit robbery; when the affair was discovered he was examined and cited Kai's words about studying his art; Kai sat in guilt and was bound in the imperial prison of the commandant of justice for two years, yet constantly recited the classics and composed a commentary on the Documents.
translations.idiomatic: Under Huandi, Pei You turned fog to banditry and implicated Zhang Kai, who spent two years in jail writing a Documents commentary.

sentences.zh: 後以事無驗，見原還家。

translations.literal: Later because the affair lacked proof he was pardoned and returned home.
translations.idiomatic: When the charge failed, he went free.

sentences.zh: 建和三年，下詔安車備禮聘之，辭以篤疾不行。

translations.literal: In the third year of Jianhe an edict ordered a comfortable carriage and full rites to summon him; he declined on grave illness and did not go.
translations.idiomatic: Jianhe 3 brought a coach summons; he pleaded grave illness.

sentences.zh: 年七十，終於家。

translations.literal: At seventy he ended at home.
translations.idiomatic: He died at home at seventy.

sentences.zh: 子陵。

translations.literal: The son was Ling.
translations.idiomatic: His son was Zhang Ling.

sentences.zh: 陵字處沖，官至尚書。

translations.literal: Ling, courtesy name Chuchong, rose in office to minister of the secretariat.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Ling, style Chuchong, became minister of the secretariat.

sentences.zh: 元嘉中，歲首朝賀，大將軍梁冀帶□入省，陵呵叱令出，□羽林、虎賁奪冀□。

translations.literal: In the Yuanjia era, at the New Year court assembly, general-in-chief Liang Ji wearing a sword entered the palace offices; Ling shouted and ordered him out, ordering the feathered forest and tiger guard to seize Ji.
translations.idiomatic: At Yuanjia New Year, Liang Ji entered court armed; Zhang Ling had guards disarm him,

sentences.zh: 冀跪謝，陵不應，即劾奏冀，請廷尉論罪，有詔以一歲俸贖，而百僚肅然。

translations.literal: Ji knelt to apologize; Ling did not answer but at once memorialized to impeach Ji and asked the commandant of justice to judge his crime; an edict ordered one year's salary as ransom, yet the hundred offices stood in awe.
translations.idiomatic: impeached him, and won a token fine that nonetheless awed the bureaucracy.

sentences.zh: 初，冀弟不疑為河南尹，舉陵孝廉。

translations.literal: At first Ji's younger brother Buyi was grand administrator of Henan and had recommended Ling as filial and incorrupt.
translations.idiomatic: Buyi had once recommended Zhang Ling filial and honest,

sentences.zh: 不疑疾陵之奏冀，因謂曰：「昔舉君，適所以自罰也。

translations.literal: Buyi resented Ling's memorial against Ji and therefore said to him: 'Formerly I recommended you—only to punish myself.'
translations.idiomatic: and said recommending him had been self-punishment,

sentences.zh: 」陵對曰：「明府不以陵不肖，誤見擢序，今申公憲，以報私恩。」

translations.literal: Ling answered: 'The enlightened prefect did not think Ling unworthy and wrongly raised him in order; now I declare the public law to repay private favor.'"
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Ling replied that he now served the law, not a patron's favor."

sentences.zh: 不疑有愧色。

translations.literal: Buyi had a shamefaced look.
translations.idiomatic: Buyi colored.

sentences.zh: 陵弟玄。

translations.literal: Ling's younger brother was Xuan.
translations.idiomatic: His brother was Zhang Xuan.

sentences.zh: 玄字處虛，沉深有才略，以時亂不仕。

translations.literal: Xuan, courtesy name Chuxu, was deep and reserved with talent and strategy; because the age was in disorder he did not take office.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Xuan, style Chuxu, stayed out of office in the chaos,

sentences.zh: 司空張溫數以禮辟，不能致。

translations.literal: Minister of works Zhang Wen several times summoned him with ritual but could not bring him in.
translations.idiomatic: though Zhang Wen repeatedly tried to hire him.

sentences.zh: 中平二年，溫以車騎將軍出征涼州賊邊章等，將行，玄自田廬被褐帶索，要說溫曰：「天下寇賊雲起，豈不以黃門常侍無道故乎？

translations.literal: In the second year of Zhongping, Wen as chariot-and-cavalry general went out to campaign against the bandits Bian Zhang and others of Liangzhou; about to depart, Xuan from his field hut in coarse cloak and rope belt intercepted Wen and persuaded him: 'Bandits and rebels rise like clouds—is it not because the yellow gate regular attendants are without the Way?
translations.idiomatic: Zhongping 2: Zhang Xuan stopped Zhang Wen's Liangzhou expedition to say the rebellion stemmed from corrupt eunuchs,

sentences.zh: 聞中貴人公卿已下當出祖道於平樂觀，明公總天下威重，握六師之要，若於中坐酒酣，鳴金鼓，整行陣，召軍正執有罪者誅之，引兵還屯都亭，以次翦除中官，解天下之倒縣，報海內之怨毒，然後顯用隱逸忠正之士，則邊章之徒宛轉股掌之上矣。

translations.literal: I hear that the inner nobles and dukes downward will go out to the farewell road at the Pingle observatory; the enlightened lord holds the awe of the realm and grasps the six hosts' pivot—if amid the feast you strike the gongs and drums, align the ranks, call the army judge and execute the guilty, lead the troops back to camp at the capital lodge, then in order cut away the inner officials, release the realm from hanging upside down, repay the sea within's bitter hatred, then openly employ reclusive loyal upright men—then men like Bian Zhang will writhe on your palm.'"
translations.idiomatic: and urged him to execute eunuchs at the Pingle send-off, free the empire, then hire good men—Bian Zhang would be trivial.

sentences.zh: 」溫聞大震，不能對，良久謂玄曰：「處虛，非不悅子之言，顧吾不能行，如何！

translations.literal: When Wen heard this he was greatly shaken and could not answer; after a long while he said to Xuan: 'Chuxu, it is not that I dislike your words—only that I cannot act on them—what can be done!'
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Wen said he dared not do it,

sentences.zh: 」玄乃歎曰：「事行則為福，不行則為賊。

translations.literal: Xuan thereupon sighed: 'If the deed is done it becomes fortune; if not done it becomes calamity.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Xuan sighed that success meant fortune, failure crime,

sentences.zh: 今與公長辭矣。

translations.literal: Now I take long farewell with the lord.'
translations.idiomatic: and bade him farewell,

sentences.zh: 」即仰藥欲飲之。

translations.literal: At once he raised poison to drink.
translations.idiomatic: then raised poison to his lips.

sentences.zh: 溫前執其手曰：「子忠於我，我不能用，是吾罪也，子何為當然！

translations.literal: Wen stepped forward and grasped his hand: 'You are loyal to me; I cannot use you—that is my fault; why should you be like this!
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Wen stayed his hand: blame me, not yourself,

sentences.zh: 且出口入耳之言，誰今知之！

translations.literal: Moreover words from mouth to ear—who will know them today!'
translations.idiomatic: and said a secret between them would stay secret."

sentences.zh: 」[一] 玄遂去，隱居魯陽山中。

translations.literal: [One] Xuan thereupon departed and lived in reclusion in the mountains of Luyang.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Xuan fled to Luyang mountains,

sentences.zh: [二]及董卓秉政，聞之，辟以為掾，舉侍御史，不就。

translations.literal: [Two] When Dong Zhuo seized power he heard of him, summoned him as clerk, recommended censor-in-attendance—he did not go.
translations.idiomatic: and when Dong Zhuo summoned him he refused,

sentences.zh: 卓臨之以兵，不得已強起，至輪氏，道病終。

translations.literal: Zhuo confronted him with troops; having no choice he rose forcibly, reached Lushi, fell ill on the road, and died.
translations.idiomatic: until Dong Zhuo forced him out; he died en route at Lushi.

sentences.zh: [三] 注[一]左傳曰：「言出於余口，入於爾耳。」

translations.literal: [Three] Note one: the Zuo commentary says: 'Words that leave my mouth enter your ear.'"
translations.idiomatic: Note: Zuo on confidential speech,

sentences.zh: 注[二]山在今汝州南。

translations.literal: Note two: the mountain is south of present Ruzhou.
translations.idiomatic: Note: Luyang range south of Ruzhou,

sentences.zh: 注[三]輪氏，縣，屬穎川郡，故城在今洛州洛陽縣城西南。

translations.literal: Note three: Lushi, a county belonging to Yingchuan commandery; the old city is southwest of Luoyang city in present Luozhou.
translations.idiomatic: Note: Lushi in Yingchuan southwest of Luoyang.

sentences.zh: 贊曰：中世儒門，賈、鄭名學。

translations.literal: The encomium says: In the middle age of the Ru gate, Jia and Zheng were famed learning.
translations.idiomatic: The verse praises Jia and Zheng schools,

sentences.zh: 觿馳一介，爭禮氈幄。

translations.literal: Zhong with one mission contended ritual in the felt tent.
translations.idiomatic: Zheng Zhong defying the Xiongnu,

sentences.zh: [一]升、元守經，義偏情較，霸貴知止，辭交戚裡。

translations.literal: [One] Sheng and Yuan guarded the classics with biased feeling; Ba knew when to stop and declined ties with powerful neighbors.
translations.idiomatic: Fan Sheng and Chen Yuan split hairs; Zhang Ba quit while ahead,

sentences.zh: 公超善術，所捨成市。

translations.literal: Gongchao was skilled in arts; where he lodged became a market.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Kai's fog arts drew crowds like a market.

sentences.zh: 注[一]一介，單使也。

translations.literal: Note one: one mission means a lone envoy.
translations.idiomatic: Note: one envoy,

sentences.zh: 左傳曰：「君亦不使一介行李告於寡君。

translations.literal: The Zuo commentary says: 'You also do not send one minor envoy with luggage to announce to our unworthy lord.'"
translations.idiomatic: Zuo on a lone messenger;

sentences.zh: 」氈幄謂匈奴也。

translations.literal: Felt tent means the Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: felt tent means the steppe court.

sentences.zh: 校勘記 一二一七頁四行使撰條例章句傳詁汲本、殿本「傳詁」作「訓詁」。

translations.literal: Collation note page 1217 line 4: ordering compilation of regulations, chapter divisions, transmitted glosses—汲 and 殿 editions read xun gu instead of zhuan gu.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: some editions read xun gu for zhuan gu in Zheng Xing's task,

sentences.zh: 今按：注專釋「詁」字，引說文「詁，訓古言也」，似正文不作「訓詁」。

translations.literal: Now considered: the commentary glosses only gu, citing Shuowen 'gu glosses ancient speech,' so the main text probably did not read xun gu.
translations.idiomatic: but the commentary supports zhuan gu,

sentences.zh: 下賈逵傳云「寫其傳詁」，亦當作「傳詁」之一證也。

translations.literal: The biography of Jia Kui below says 'copied its transmitted glosses,' which is further evidence it should be zhuan gu.
translations.idiomatic: and Jia Kui's "transmitted glosses" supports zhuan gu.

sentences.zh: 一二一八頁一０行*[囂]*虛心禮請據刊誤及校補說補。

translations.literal: Page 1218 line 10: Ao *modestly* courteously invited—supplied per 刊誤 and collation supplement.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: Wei Ao's modest invitation text restored.

sentences.zh: 一二一八頁一０行囂鄉己自飾汲本、殿本「鄉」作「矜」。

translations.literal: Page 1218 line 10: Ao xiang ji self-adorned—汲 and 殿 read jin for xiang.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: xiang versus jin in Wei Ao's self-adornment,

sentences.zh: 按：段注說文依漢石經論語，改「矜」為「鄉」，雲□矛令聲，則以作「鄉」為是。

translations.literal: Considered: Duan's Shuowen note follows Han stone classics Analects, changing jin to xiang, explaining mao ling sound—so writing xiang is correct.
translations.idiomatic: with philological note on jin/xiang,

sentences.zh: 然紹興本「鄉」「矜」互見，前後亦不一致也。

translations.literal: Yet Shaoxing editions alternate xiang and jin, not consistent front and back.
translations.idiomatic: Shaoxing prints vary.

sentences.zh: 一二一九頁八行諸侯不期而至者八百人按：汲本、殿本「至」作「會」。

translations.literal: Page 1219 line 8: eight hundred lords without prior appointment arrived—汲 and 殿 read hui for zhi.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: "assembled" versus "arrived" at Mengjin,

sentences.zh: 一二二０頁四行促為辨裝汲本、殿本「辨」作「辦」。

translations.literal: Page 1220 line 4: hastened to prepare travel gear—汲 and 殿 read ban for bian.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: ban versus bian for travel preparations,

translations.literal: Considered: bian originally written as [variant], bian phonetic.
translations.idiomatic: philological note on bian/ban,

sentences.zh: 段玉裁謂俗作「辨」，為辨別字，別作□力之「辦」，為干辦字，實則古□別、干□無二義，亦無二音二形也。

translations.literal: Duan Yucai says vulgar writing uses bian for distinction and li li's ban for handling—actually ancient bian did not split meaning, sound, or form.
translations.idiomatic: Duan Yucai on graphic variants,

sentences.zh: 一二二四頁二行建安中改作「聞」按：集解引沉欽韓說，謂閿字本作「闅」，建安中改作「閿」，非改作「聞」也。

translations.literal: Page 1224 line: Jian'an era changed to wen—集解 cites Shen Qinhan: the place name was originally 闅, changed in Jian'an to 閿, not changed to wen.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: Wenxiang place name graph,

sentences.zh: 一二二四頁一三行侷促狐疑「侷促」原作「局足」，逕據汲本、殿本改。

translations.literal: Page 1224 line 13: ju cu hesitant—original read ju zu, emended per 汲 and 殿.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: ju cu for hesitant,

sentences.zh: 按：此鼎韻謰語，通常皆作「侷促」也。

translations.literal: Considered: this is a rhyming binome, usually written ju cu.
translations.idiomatic: note on binome spelling,

sentences.zh: 一二二七頁三行胡貊守關按：刊誤謂「關」當作「闕」。

translations.literal: Page 1227 line 3: Hu mo guard pass—刊誤 says guan should be que.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: gate versus palace gate in Fan Sheng,

sentences.zh: 方喻迫近，不當雲關。

translations.literal: The metaphor is pressing near; it should not say guan.
translations.idiomatic: editorial argument,

sentences.zh: 一二二九頁一行萬事理按：張森楷校勘記謂惠校本「事」作「物」，補注引劉向說苑亦作「物」。

translations.literal: Page 1229 line 1: wan wu ordered—張森楷 collation says Huixiao edition reads wu for shi; 補注 cites Liu Xiang Shuoyuan also has wu.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: "things" versus "affairs" in Changes gloss,

sentences.zh: 一二三三頁六行賓臣者霸按：集解引沉欽韓說，謂袁宏紀作「賓臣者王」。

translations.literal: Page 1233 line 6: guest ministers hegemon—集解 cites Shen Qinhan: Yuan Hong ji reads wang for ba.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: wang versus ba in Chen Yuan memorial,

sentences.zh: 一二三六頁二行使發出左氏傳大義長於二傳者汲本、殿本無「發」字。

translations.literal: Page 1236 line 2: ordered to set forth Zuo great meanings excelling the two transmissions—汲 and 殿 lack fa.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: presence of fa in Jia Kui edict,

sentences.zh: 按：殿本考證謂監本「出」字上有「發」字。

translations.literal: Considered: Dian edition 考证 says supervisory edition has fa above chu.
translations.idiomatic: Palace edition adds fa,

sentences.zh: 一二三六頁一五行曷*

translations.literal: Page 1236 line 15: he—
translations.idiomatic: Collation: damaged he in Gongyang,

translations.literal: (wei)
translations.idiomatic: gloss wei,

sentences.zh: **[為]*通濫據汲本、殿本改，與今公羊傳合。

translations.literal: **[wei]* tong lan—emended per Ji and Dian editions, matching the present Gongyang commentary.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: the lacunose Gongyang line on "communicating Lan" was restored from Ji and Dian recensions to match the received Gongyang.

sentences.zh: 一二四０頁七行郁字叔異集解引沉欽韓說，謂文選四十六注引東觀記作「字幼異」。

translations.literal: Page 1240 line 7: Yu's courtesy name Shuyi—集解 cites Shen Qinhan: Wen xuan commentary 46 cites Dongguan ji as "courtesy name Youyi."
translations.idiomatic: Collation: Yu Yu's style is given as Shuyi here; Shen Qinhan notes the Wen xuan commentary cites Dongguan ji as Youyi.

sentences.zh: 按：今聚珍本東觀記亦作「字叔異」。

translations.literal: Considered: the present Juzhen edition of Dongguan ji also reads "courtesy name Shuyi."
translations.idiomatic: The Juzhen Dongguan ji agrees on Shuyi.

sentences.zh: 一二四一頁九行長水校尉樊*

translations.literal: Page 1241 line 9: colonel of the Chang waters Fan—
translations.idiomatic: Collation: fragment naming Fan as colonel of the Chang water regiment,

translations.literal: (shu)
translations.idiomatic: with alternate reading shu,

sentences.zh: **[鯈]*據樊宏傳改。

translations.literal: **[tiao] emended according to the biography of Fan Hong.
translations.idiomatic: restored as Fan Shu per Fan Hong's biography.

sentences.zh: 下同。

translations.literal: The same below.
translations.idiomatic: The same emendation applies below.

sentences.zh: 一二四二頁四行□我戟按：王先謙謂類聚十五引續漢書作「棄子戟」。

translations.literal: Page 1242 line 4: □ my halberd—Wang Xianqian says Taiping yulan 15 cites the Xu Han shu as "cast aside my halberd."
translations.idiomatic: Collation: the damaged first verb in the children's rhyme; Wang Xianqian cites Yulan's Xu Han as qi zi ji "cast aside my halberd."

sentences.zh: 一二四二頁一一行中子楷按：「楷」原作「揩」，據汲本、殿本改正。

translations.literal: Page 1242 line 11: middle son Kai—originally written kai with hand radical; corrected per Ji and Dian editions.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: Zhang Kai's name was miswritten with the "hand" radical and fixed in standard editions,

sentences.zh: 下同。

translations.literal: The same below.
translations.idiomatic: with the same correction carried through below.

sentences.zh: 一二四四頁一０行且出口入耳之言誰今知之王先謙謂「今」當為「令」之誤文，言出於子口，入於我耳，我不言，誰令他人知之，語意自順。

translations.literal: Page 1244 line 10: moreover words from mouth to ear—who today knows them—Wang Xianqian says jin should be ling in error: words leave your son's mouth and enter my ear; if I do not speak, who orders others to know—thus the sense runs smoothly.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: Wang Xianqian emends "who today knows" to "who could make known" (jin to ling) for smoother sense of a secret between two men.

sentences.zh: 今按：今猶即也，則也，言誰則知之，王說未諦。

translations.literal: Now considered: jin here means "then" or "namely"; "who then would know"—Wang's reading is not decisive.
translations.idiomatic: The editor rejects Wang's emendation: jin can mean "then," so the received text still reads well.

sentences.zh: 一二四四頁一一行至輪氏按：「輪」續志同，前志作「綸」。

translations.literal: Page 1244 line 11: reaching Lushi—"lun" matches the Xu zhi; the former treatise writes lun with silk radical.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: the county name Lushi matches Hou Han zhi; Hanshu writes the homograph with silk radical.
